title: Pathfinders
description: FIRST-TIME ATTENDEE RESOURCE: Pathfinders will be available to help you find your way. Pathfinders is a volunteer group dedicated to mentoring first-time conference visitors. The Pathfinders team can offer detailed explanations and descriptions of all SIGGRAPH programs or venues, and help you build a schedule based on your personal skills and interests.

title: Women of SIGGRAPH Conversations (WOSC) Breakfast Mixer
description: Join Women of SIGGRAPH Conversations (WOSC) for breakfast and an interactive, forward-looking session focused on capability building across creative and technical careers. Through dynamic ice breakers and collaborative discussion, we’ll explore how people develop range, build and signal competencies, and navigate growth through upskilling, lateral moves, and strategic pivots. We’ll talk honestly about generalist versus T-shaped paths, personal branding, and how tools like AI are reshaping how we learn, work, and expand what we can do. Bring your own questions, experiences, and ambitions, and leave with practical frameworks, concrete ideas, and a clearer sense of how to intentionally build capability for the next phase of your career.

title: AI for Creative Visual Content Generation Editing and Understanding (CVEU 2026)
description: This course introduces a multi-agent framework fusing Generative AI’s creativity with Computer Graphics’ precision. Addressing GenAI’s limitations in data accuracy, we demonstrate how agents orchestrate deterministic rendering (e.g., for charts or spatial logic) to guide video synthesis. Attendees will learn to build systems for controllable, precise cinematic and data storytelling.

title: An Introduction to Neural Shading
description: Learn neural shading from the ground up. This hands-on course teaches attendees to build and train MLPs directly in Slang shaders, leverage GPU tensor cores for hardware acceleration, and implement neural materials — transforming complex visual phenomena into compact, high-fidelity learned representations.

title: Asiagraphics Workshop on Neural Graphics Processing
description: Neural graphics is transforming how we create, represent, and interact with visual content. This workshop brings together leading researchers across geometry, rendering, generative modeling, and embodied AI to present a unified view of this rapidly evolving field.Through a curated sequence of invited talks, the program will guide attendees from geometric foundations and 3D generation to neural rendering, real-time techniques, physics-aware reconstruction, and interaction with the physical world. The primary objective is to explore how neural representations and generative models can be integrated with classical graphics pipelines to enable more scalable, controllable, and physically grounded visual computing. The workshop will feature the technical presentations, encouraging active engagement between speakers and attendees. Participants can expect lively exchanges, opportunities to ask questions, and insights into emerging research directions. Designed for researchers, practitioners, and students in graphics, vision, and AI, this workshop offers a concise yet comprehensive overview of neural graphics and its future trajectory.

title: Frontier Workshops: Digital Twins for Science and Industry
description: This workshop addresses the transformative role of digital twins across science and industry. Following the National Academies' 2024 standardized definition, we seek to foster a community of practice embracing this common understanding across diverse domains. We open with a 2026 state-of-the-art survey and Digital Twin Consortium perspectives on evolving standards, then examine societal impacts in healthcare, fusion energy, climate science, and sovereign AI. Technical sessions address rendering fidelity in virtual replicas, AI integration for prediction and optimization, and frameworks combining machine learning with physics-based simulation. Case studies span drug discovery, computational biomedicine, sustainable energy, entertainment production, and scientific visualization.

title: Optimal Transport for Fluid Simulation: New and Old
description: This course on Fluid Simulation focuses on Optimal Transport, a mathematical toolbox for creating a 3D mesh representation that follows the movement of the fluid while accurately ensuring volume conservation, fluid-boundary interactions and free-surface evolution. For a hands-on experience, it includes many bring-back-home programming examples, from simple to elaborate.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH: Opportunities and Programs for Educators - Part 1
description: The kickoff of the 2026 SIGGRAPH Educators Program will include an overview of activities and opportunities with, and sponsored by, the ACM SIGGRAPH Education Committee. Additionally, the winning entries from the SpaceTime competition will be shown along with a screening of the show reel for the 2026 double-curated Faculty Submitted Student Work (FSSW) exhibit. Designed as a way for educators to share their project ideas across schools and disciplines, FSSW is an online archive of assignment and project briefs as well as a curated video emphasizing the variety of student work and schools submitted. The annual exhibition video is a taste of the content available on the Education Committee website and this event showcases and celebrates to the greater SIGGRAPH community the best examples of student work done for projects and assignments for the 2025-2026 school year. Part 2 of this event will begin immediately upon the conclusion of presentations in Part 1.

title: Beyond the Dot: New Ways to Reduce Motion Sickness in Gaming
description: Motion sickness remains a significant barrier to immersive games, affecting up to one-third of players. We present a player-centered mitigation approach developed within a kinetic first-person game, reframing motion comfort as accessibility and embedding inclusive design directly into gameplay systems, supported by empirical adoption and impact data.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH: Opportunities and Programs for Educators -– Part 2
description: This session is the second part of the kickoff for the 2026 SIGGRAPH Educators Program and will primarily be the screening of the annual show reel for the 2026 double-curated Faculty Submitted Student Work (FSSW) exhibit. Designed as a way for educators to share their project ideas across schools and disciplines, FSSW is an online archive of assignment and project briefs as well as a curated video emphasizing the variety of student work and schools submitted. The annual exhibition video is a taste of the content available on the Education Committee website and this event showcases and celebrates to the greater SIGGRAPH community the best examples of student work done for projects and assignments for the 2025-2026 school year. NOTE: Part 2 will begin immediately upon completion of other presentations by the ACM SIGGRAPH Education Committee that begins in Part 1.

title: The Connective Tissue: VFX Supervision Across Art, Camera, and Creatures | VES Women Who Lead Series
description: Visual Effects Supervisor Tara DeMarco will host a discussion of VFX Supervision as a discipline of deep collaboration with production design and cinematography. We invite questions about how invisible, craft-driven VFX work serves story without overwhelming it. We can examine together how early cross-departmental integration can bring the most fantastical work to life in a grounded way.

title: Advancing Virtual Production: Technical Innovations and Collaborative Pipelines for Film, TV & Live Events
description: Exploring recent advances in virtual production resulting in higher performance, better data interchange, and realtime VP scalability for VFX supervisors and filmmakers. From lumenAOVs to frame generation with nDisplay and fast rendering Gaussian Splats, speakers discuss the integration of Epic and NVIDIA technologies, technical opportunities, pipeline impacts, and collaborative workflows. Moderator Michael Stein Journey Panelists Lon Grohs NVIDIA Ryan Mayeda Epic Games Markus Hoell Dimension Studio Paul Franklin BeloFX Callum Macmillan Dimension Studio

title: Curves for Technical Artists
description: This course is an introduction to curves for technical artists and aspiring graphics engineers. We will build an understanding of curves used in computer graphics, especially rigging, from simple mathematical notation, using graphical visualisations, code implementation and relating them to their use in DCCs.

title: Game Audio Lasagna: Building Layers to Create New Sonic Worlds
description: Join a Composer and Sound Designer as they share a glimpse into how they build sonic palettes and craft unique audio experiences for games. Hear examples of how various layers of different sounds can create a fireball spell, which unique combination of instruments create an unsettling atmosphere, or even how to evoke a specific emotion within just a few seconds of audio. In addition to diving into the details on how these sound effects and musical excerpts were made, we’ll also focus on the high level decisions that would inform the audio’s direction. Analyzing how to determine that which is most important for audio to represent, aligning across multiple teams on creative goals, and other essential considerations for the production of audio for games. Submitter Kole Hicks Kole Hicks Music Panelists Kole Hicks Kole Hicks Music Emmanuel Lagumbay Sword and Sound Audio

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Nsight Graphics in Action: Develop and Debug Modern Ray-Tracing Applications
description: Dive into the world of high-performance graphics programming with this hands-on lab focused on developing and debugging real-time rendering and ray tracing applications built with Vulkan and Direct3D 12. This lab teaches how to effectively use NVIDIA Nsight Graphics to inspect and debug frames to identify and diagnose common rendering bugs and performance blockers. By the end of the lab participants will understand the direct connection between the tools and the graphics runtime, and will be prepared to use Nsight Graphics with their own applications. Key tools covered include the Graphics Debugger, Ray Tracing Inspector, Shader Debugger, and GPU Crash Dump Inspector. Presenters Jonathan Litt NVIDIA Jeffrey Kiel NVIDIA Jacob Skarphol NVIDIA

title: We're All In This Together: The Making Of Disney and Pixar's Hoppers
description: Creating the world of Hoppers required reconciling three potentially incompatible visual goals: the graphic clarity needed for broad comedy, the controlled lighting and staging required for suspense, and the rich, observational beauty of nature photography. Rather than choosing between stylization and realism, the filmmakers built a collaborative, cross-departmental pipeline that allowed physical behavior and artistic design to coexist in every shot. This production session presents the making of that hybrid visual language and the teamwork that made it possible. Artists and technologists from production design, sets, layout, characters, crowds, FX, simulation, lighting, compositing, and tools worked in tight feedback loops to build a hybrid visual language where physical simulation anchors the world, and artistically directed stylization shapes how we see it. Presenters Beth Albright Visual Effects Supervisor Pixar Animation Studios Ian Megibben Director of Photography Pixar Animation Studios Jon Reisch FX Supervisor Pixar Animation Studios Andrea Goh Layout Lead Pixar Animation Studios Christina Garcia Weiland Set Dress Lead Pixar Animation Studios Laurie Kim Sim Lead Pixar Anima

title: ‘Battlefield 6’: How We Brought The Franchise Vision To Life With Our Destruction System Debut
description: Battlefield 6 redefines destruction as a tactical ecosystem rather than visual spectacle. Through ten interrelated destruction elements, systemic and pre-simulated workflows, consistent material damage classes, and shared pipelines, destruction reshapes gameplay spaces, enhances player agency, and delivers immersive, readable, and scalable battlefield experiences that fulfills franchise vision.

title: AI-Driven Data Pipelines for Digital Twins and Simulation
description: AI is transforming how digital twins and simulations are built, fed, and maintained but connecting intelligent pipelines across fragmented tools and formats remains a hard, unsolved problem. This facilitated discussion brings together practitioners from film, games, architecture, and engineering to share real-world experience building AI-powered data pipelines: automated ingestion, anomaly detection, model-driven transformation, and live feedback loops between simulation and physical systems. What is working in production? Where does AI add genuine value versus hype? Come exchange honest lessons and push the conversation forward on what AI-driven pipeline infrastructure actually looks like at scale.

title: DAC & SIGGRAPH 2026 Creative Content Overview
description: This session offers a preview of SIGGRAPH 2026’s creative programming. Chairs and Directors from across the conference will share highlights from their programs, including featured projects, upcoming events, and curatorial directions. Attendees will gain an early view of how this year’s conference is expanding the role of digital art, encouraging cross-disciplinary exchange, and supporting experimentation across media, technology, and artistic practice. The session also introduces the ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community (DAC), a platform for global dialogue among artists, designers, and technologists. Through initiatives such as SPARKS Lightning Talks, curated digital exhibitions, and student competitions, DAC connects artistic practice with computer graphics, interactive media, and emerging technologies. More information is available at dac.siggraph.org.

title: Animation Theater 1: Open Worlds
description: What does it mean to build a world from nothing? This program brings together short films that take the art and philosophy of video game world-building seriously — from sweeping open-world epics to the quiet intimacy of indie landscapes.

title: We're All In This Together: The Making Of Disney and Pixar's Hoppers - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: DevOps in Production
description: Production companies are often production-driven rather than engineering-driven. Because of this, they have a particularly strong need to leverage DevOps technologies and automation in order to efficiently deliver and maintain technologies within production pipelines. This BOF will focus on recent advances in DevOps, from coding standards and workflow automation to the growing impact of AI-based workflows and tools. We are especially interested in how these technologies are being adopted in production studios and companies today. The goal of this session is to share production-proven knowledge and tips and tricks that help improve the delivery of technologies in production environments.

title: Performance Capture at Scale: Building a Pipeline for Battlefield 6 Cinematics
description: Film-grade virtual production workflows are increasingly finding their way onto game MoCap stages — but adapting them to the pace, scale, and constraints of AAA production is its own challenge. This talk details how EA Create Capture met that challenge for Battlefield 6: from 1-to-1 physical set construction and moving vehicle builds to real-time visualization that enabled key creative decisions on set and same-day editorial delivery. Submitter Pasha Sol Electronic Arts Panelist Pasha Sol Electronic Arts

title: Between the Frames

title: Do it, Right Now!

title: Frontiers Workshop: Graphics In Medicine: From the Dev Floor to the Operating Room 2
description: We're back for round 2 of "From the Dev Floor to the Operating Room"! Like last year, we hope to bring to light more of the wonderful applications of computer graphics in medicine. We'll be discussing current challenges with XR in medicine, digital twins, simulation, robotics and more. We'll also expand on last years workshop activity with a brand new panel of physicians to help us practice designing graphics solutions for medicine that actually make an impact. This will also be a great place to network with people interested the beautiful intersection of Medicine and Technology

title: In Conversation with Tyree Dillihay
description: Making his feature directorial debut with Sony Pictures Animation, Tyree Dillihay joins us to look back on the making of "GOAT," the story of a small goat with big dreams who gets a once-in-a-lifetime shot to join the pros and play roarball, a high-intensity, co-ed, full-contact sport dominated by the fastest, fiercest animals in the world. Blending a unique, original visual style with the kinetic energy of modern basketball culture, the film is a love letter to the underdog spirit. In this conversation, Dillihay will discuss bringing his vision to life with an A-list voice cast led by Caleb McLaughlin and Gabrielle Union, working with producer Stephen Curry and other NBA legends including Andre Iguodala to achieve authentic roarball action, and what it took to make his feature debut at one of animation's most celebrated studios. Creators Garth Burkhard Senior Vice President of Publicity & Digita Sony Pictures Animation Tyree Dillihay Director Sony Pictures Animation

title: Lines and Minds: Visual Abstraction in Art, Psychology, and Computer Graphics
description: Visual abstraction --- from sketches and diagrams to icons and comics --- enables people to communicate, express, and create by distilling complex visual information into simplified forms that preserve essential meaning. These simplified forms support perception, reasoning, and communication in ways that detailed depiction often cannot. Visual abstraction has been studied across cognitive science, art theory, and computer graphics, offering complementary perspectives on how humans perceive, produce, and interpret such representations. This workshop brings together these perspectives to examine abstraction as a cognitive mechanism, a communicative tool, and a computational challenge, exploring questions such as: Why and how do people abstract? What makes a simplification effective? How can machines learn to abstract in ways aligned with human perception? And as AI excels at generating realistic images, what role might abstraction play in how humans and AI create together?

title: NVIDIA Graphics Developer Tools Roadmap: Agentic AI and Beyond (First Offering)
description: This presentation offers a focused update on the state of NVIDIA's graphics developer tools and a look at how agentic AI workflows are shaping their future direction. Covering recent advances in Nsight Graphics, Nsight Systems, and Nsight Aftermath, the session highlights key improvements to debugging, profiling, and crash analysis capabilities alongside an exploration of where AI-driven automation is heading next. Attendees will come away with a grounded understanding of the current tooling landscape and practical insight into the agentic AI features being built for graphics developers. This presentation is paired with two hands-on labs where participants will directly use and explore AI-assisted workflows. Presenters Aurelio Reis NVIDIA Jonathan Litt NVIDIA

title: NVIDIA: Beyond 3D: From Real-World Objects to Digital Assets and Physical AI
description: Discover how scalable compute, advanced graphics and generative AI reshape scientific and cultural narratives. Leveraging AI and neural graphics, we guide attendees through the complete object lifecycle - Seeing, Reconstructing, Restoring, and Simulating. Learn to build active, physically grounded digital twins that unlock deep insights across scientific and cultural domains.

title: We Never Go Out of Style
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Early Career Development: Kickstart a Career in Computer Graphics – Resume, Reel & Industry Insights
description: The ACM SIGGRAPH Early Career Development Committee (ECDC) Resume and Reel Review program has long supported students and emerging professionals through mentorship, portfolio feedback, and year-round career development programming. In this interactive panel discussion, ECDC brings together industry professionals from fashion, enterprise visualization, infrastructure, product development, and creative technology to discuss the many pathways available within modern 3D careers beyond traditional entertainment pipelines. Panelists include leaders working in apparel visualization, immersive media, enterprise 3D workflows, AI-assisted production, and digital product creation. Together, they will share practical insights into resumes and demo reels, hiring expectations, portfolio strategy, networking, mentorship, and adapting creative skills across evolving industries and technologies. Rather than a traditional lecture, this session is designed as an interactive conversation shaped by audience questions and discussion. Attendees will have the opportunity to engage directly with panelists on topics including career transitions, breaking into new industries, building sustainable creative ca

title: Forging the Glacier at IO Interactive: Fireside Chat with Henrik Schlichter (Technical Director)
description: As the game industry grapples with rising costs, shifting platforms, and an explosion of available tools, the question of how studios build their games has never been more consequential. IO Interactive Technical Director Henrik Schlichter sits down to discuss, debate, and unpack the realities of modern game development - what it takes to build the custom Glacier engine, innovate at scale, and how to build cheaper, faster and better without sacrificing quality. Equal parts technical deep-dive and industry reality check, this fireside chat sets the stage for bold conversations for Games at SIGGRAPH 2026. Submitter Henrik Schlichter IO Interactive Panelists Henrik Schlichter IO Interactive John Canning Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Debugging with Nsight Graphics (First Offering)
description: Modern graphics applications are getting increasingly difficult to debug. Ray Tracing, advanced rendering pipelines with neural rendering, GPU crashes, and performance bottlenecks often require developers to navigate large amounts of data before identifying the true source of a problem. At the same time, AI-powered tools are beginning to transform how developers investigate and understand complex systems. In this hands-on lab, participants will enter the Hall of Mysteries, an interactive graphics debugging experience built around a series of realistic rendering and performance challenges. Using a preview of the in-development Nsight Graphics MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, participants will study a Vulkan application to investigate visual artifacts, analyze renderer behavior, diagnose GPU crashes, and optimize performance using professional graphics debugging workflows. Rather than following a scripted tutorial or traditional use of the tooling, participants will gather evidence, formulate hypotheses, inspect captures and traces, and verify conclusions through AI-guided investigation. Along the way, they will learn how AI-assisted workflows can accelerate debugging while prese

title: Beyond Eyeballing: The Need for Standardized Assessment in AI-Driven Rendering
description: The shift from native graphics rendering to AI-driven neural rendering (NVIDIA-DLSS, AMD-FSR, Intel-XeSS) has fundamentally disrupted how the video game industry measures visual fidelity. Traditional objective metrics no longer suffice, leaving developers, QA testers, and reviewers to rely on subjective "eyeballing". Following the enthusiastic response to our 2025 BoF, we return to continue the conversation. This session will explore the fragmentation of quality assessment in modern game development workflows, the severe economic costs of manual visual QA, and how the interactive entertainment industry can learn from established telecommunications standards (such as ITU and VQEG) to build automated, objective quality metrics.

title: SIGGRAPH for Beginners - Welcome to the Conference!
description: New to SIGGRAPH? Returning after a SIGGRAPH-break? Just curious about what the conference has to offer? If your answer is YES to any of these questions, this session is your friendly introduction to the week ahead. SIGGRAPH for Beginners will help you make the most of your time at the conference. You'll get practical tips, helpful context, and a chance to ask questions in a relaxed setting. Topics include: - How to navigate the conference and its program - Where to find help and support during the week - Top Programs you won’t want to miss - An introduction to ACM SIGGRAPH, the organization behind the event Bring your questions and curiosity—and stay until the end for our Meet & Greet, featuring drinks and snacks, to kick off the conference in style! NOTE: The session will take place from 3:30 - 4:30 pm in 404 AB. The Meet & Greet from 4:30 - 5:00 pm in 409 AB.

title: An Introduction to Quantum Computing
description: Quantum computing is a radically new and exciting approach to programming. By exploiting the unusual behavior of quantum objects, this new technology invites us to re-imagine the computer graphics methods we know and love in revolutionary new ways. This course is math-free and requires no technical background.

title: Don't Do it in Post
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Fluid Simulation with the Lattice Boltzmann Method
description: Lattice Boltzmann Method (LBM) offers a powerful alternative to traditional Navier-Stokes solvers, using mesoscopic particle distributions on Cartesian lattices to achieve far superior GPU performance and accuracy. This 1.5h course introduces LBM fundamentals and recent advances in multiphase flows, free-surface flows, high–Reynolds-number flows, moving boundaries, and robotics. We also highlight applications of LBM in industrial simulation, game production, and visual effects.

title: How To Build End-To-End Physical AI Systems for Robots
description: This course covers building end-to-end physical AI for humanoid and general robots. Covers data generation with NVIDIA Cosmos (Predict, Transfer) and synthetic data; training with Isaac Lab (RL/imitation) and GR00T VLAs; and deployment on the edge with Jetson Thor. Instructors: Edith Llontop, Akul Santhosh.

title: The OpenUSD Roundtable: Using an Open Source File Format in Film and Game Dev Pipelines
description: OpenUSD has long been used to great success in the movie and VFX industries. The game development world is starting to use it too, but is reckoning with workflows that were built for movies rather than games. How have studios reconciled these differences, if at all, and what can we learn from both film and games that helps us all make better 3D workflows? This is a roundtable in which everyone who wants to can participate to discuss their experiences with OpenUSD in their pipelines, as well as those who want to ask questions about whether they should use OpenUSD in their pipeline. Questions can be submitted during the roundtable. For example, these may include topics such as: 1. What are issues you have bumped into when implementing USD into your pipeline? 2. What would be required for USD to be more widely adopted? 3. How can studios, and vendors, best share Schemas? Submitter Robin-Yann Storm RYStorm Panelists Robin-Yann Storm RYStorm Ezra Goss Naughty Dog

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Profiling with Nsight Systems (First Offering)
description: This training lab offers graphics developers a look at the agentic future being built into Nsight Systems, with a focus on holistic analysis of CPU and GPU performance. Participants will work through hands-on scenarios where AI agents assist with performance analysis . The session bridges the gap between emerging model capabilities and the practical realities of real-time and offline graphics pipelines. Developers at all levels will gain actionable insight into how agentic AI workflows can be adopted today and what innovations are on the horizon. Presenters Dan Binyamin NVIDIA Uri Shomroni NVIDIA

title: Research to Reality: Scaling Generative AI for The Wizard of Oz at Sphere
description: Adapting the 1939 classic The Wizard of Oz for the 16K x 16K immersive canvas of Sphere presented a unique technical paradox: how to translate nearly century-old, 4:3 analog footage into a fully immersive environment. Initiated at a time when viable generative video models did not yet exist, and requiring bespoke solutions beyond the capabilities of emerging commercial tools, this session reveals the collaborative R&D workflow between Sphere Studios, Magnopus, and Google DeepMind. Panelists will dissect the hybrid production pipeline used to solve the project's most complex challenges: Generative Super Resolution, Character Outpainting, and AI Performance Generation. First, the panel will address the image fidelity challenge. On Sphere’s media plane, the original film grain appeared as massive visual noise. The team will detail the development of novel upscaling architectures designed to fundamentally modernize the footage. We will demonstrate how these custom models suppressed legacy artifacts and synthesized new high-frequency detail, creating sharpness and texture comparable to modern digital cinema, while maintaining the temporal coherence required for large-format playback. Se

title: SIGGRAPH for Beginners - Meet & Greet
description: The SIGGRAPH for Beginners - Meet & Greet is a continuation of the session that will help you learn and navigate the conference like a pro. Featuring delicious snacks, this is the way to kick off the conference in style! Special thanks to our donors: Das Element (Germany) and Animationsinstitut of Filmakademie Baden-Wuerttemberg (Germany).

title: Machine Choreographies: AI, Dance, and the Future of Embodied Performance
description: Machine Choreographies: AI, Dance, and the Future of Embodied Performance explores how artificial intelligence is reshaping movement, creativity, and the body in motion. This Birds of a Feather session invites artists, technologists, and researchers to exchange ideas, tools, and provocations around co-creation with machines. From live performance capture to GenAI video, generative choreography, responsive environments, and embodied interaction, we will discuss emerging practices and critical questions. How does AI change authorship, presence, and expression in dance? What new aesthetics and ethics emerge? Join this informal gathering to share projects, spark collaborations, and imagine future forms of hybrid human-machine performance.

title: XRHeads, IRL: Connecting Young Creators, Builders, and Researchers to Hack New Realities
description: Are you an 18- to 20-something interested in virtual, augmented, or extended reality? Do you want to build the future of spatial computing with your own hands and headset? Come join us for non-alcoholic drinks and discussion on experiential technology, from open-source to academia to industry. Mocktails on the table, no topic off the table. This event is sponsored by Intercollegiate XR (ICXR) and Reality Hack Inc.

title: Research to Reality: Scaling Generative AI for The Wizard of Oz at Sphere - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

Whether you are an artist, designer, researcher, student, or simply curious, come spend an evening with fellow creatives exchanging ideas, stories, and inspiration. Together, we’ll celebrate the diverse art practices that make SIGGRAPH 2026 so dynamic—welcoming new voices, reconnecting with old friends, and enjoying the energy of a shared creative community. Don't forget to wear your conference badge! Event Meeting Location: Hotel Per La, Autograph Collection, 649 S Olive St

title: Taiwan Night (a.k.a. the Taipei SIGGRAPH Reunion)
description: Taiwan Night (a.k.a. the Taipei ACM SIGGRAPH Chapter Reunion) is an informal gathering for researchers, artists, designers, students, and professionals interested in Taiwan's computer graphics, XR, AI, digital art, and creative technology communities. Supported by Taipei ACM SIGGRAPH, it's a chance to connect with Taiwanese colleagues, exchange ideas, and learn about developments from academia, industry, and startups. All are welcome. Join us at Prank Bar (1100 S Hope St, Los Angeles, CA 90015), starting at 21:15 (PT) for RSVP'd guests and open to everyone after 23:00 (PT).

title: Frontier Talk: Write Once, Read ALWAYS: Reading Computer Graphics through Critical Code Studies
description: Most programmers don't want people looking too closely at their code, except the parts where they found that clever hack or ingenious workaround. And now with AI some people don't even read the code they generate. But what if we looked closer at code? This talk introduces Critical Code Studies, a field that uses computer source code as an entrypoint for discussing culture, philosophy, and the way we see and represent the world. Focusing on examples from the world of computer graphics, I will explore what your code may say about you and about the world you are modeling and envisioning.

title: Immersive Visualisation for Science, Research, Art and Digital Twins Applications
description: Traditionally (and since 2014), this session is about immersive visualization systems, software, and tools for science, research, scientific visualization, information visualization, art, design and digital twins. Invited speakers and panelists discuss newest initiatives and developments in immersive space as applied to data exploration, scientific discoveries, and more. Detailed agenda can be found under https://vis-bof.matters.today

title: Pathfinders
description: FIRST-TIME ATTENDEE RESOURCE: Pathfinders will be available to help you find your way. Pathfinders is a volunteer group dedicated to mentoring first-time conference visitors. The Pathfinders team can offer detailed explanations and descriptions of all SIGGRAPH programs or venues, and help you build a schedule based on your personal skills and interests.

title: A Whirlwind Introduction to Computer Graphics
description: For a beginner, walking into SIGGRAPH is a very intimidating experience. There is so much to see and so much to do, but everyone seems to be speaking an unfamiliar language. No matter who you are, this course is for you! It will give everyone a background in concepts needed to get more from the conference.

title: Arm Create - The New Arm Developer Experience
description: Join Arm to build, experiment, and bring ideas to life at Arm Create at SIGGRAPH 2026. Neural graphics on mobile are rapidly moving from research into real-world development workflows. At Arm Create Dev Day at SIGGRAPH, developers can explore what that means in practice. Bringing together developers, researchers, and ecosystem partners, the event features technical talks, guided demonstrations, and direct access to Arm experts working at the intersection of graphics, AI, and mobile development. Attendees will experience Neural Dawn, firsthand and learn how to start building with Neural Graphics today: From integrating neural rendering techniques into existing pipelines to preparing ML + GPU workflows for next-generation mobile hardware. Arm Create Dev Day showcases what developers can build now and how upcoming Arm technologies will expand future possibilities. The conversation continues throughout SIGGRAPH with additional demos, meetings, technical sessions, and stage presentations throughout the week. Presenters Peter Hodges Arm Sergio Alapont Arm Lukas Medek Sumo Digital Zhou Jian Tencent Games Li Guangyao Tencent Games Ning Liu NetEase Games Yuwen Wu NetEase Games Gabriel Peter

title: Beyond Computational Design: Design Intelligence and the Future of Creative Systems
description: Computational design reshaped interactive media, product design, and architecture over the past two decades. What comes next? This SIGGRAPH 365 session puts that question to the ACM SIGGRAPH Design Affinity Group, tool-makers and educators who have shaped computational design practice and its platforms. In a sequence of short provocations, panelists will stake out what supersedes computational design: where AI, generative methods, and open frameworks are taking creative practice, and how future tools can support human agency and collaborative forms of design. The talks will open a discussion on bridging domains: interaction and UX designers, visual and graphic designers, industrial and product designers, as well as architects, have been part of SIGGRAPH since its inception, but have remained at its edges. The Design Affinity Group exists to move them closer to the center. We want your take on what advocacy for design within SIGGRAPH should look like.

title: Digital Humans & Virtual Try-On

title: Frontiers Workshop: Quantum Machine Learning: Bridging Quantum Computing & Machine Learning
description: Quantum Machine Learning (QML) is emerging as a transformative field connecting quantum computing with modern machine learning and AI. This workshop explores how quantum-based approaches can support new learning paradigms, hybrid quantum–classical architectures, generative models, and computer graphics pipelines. Emphasizing both theory and practice, the workshop will examine near-term quantum hardware, deployable system design, and rigorous evaluation methods for identifying meaningful quantum advantage. Through three keynote talks, a panel discussion, open dialogue, and roadmap synthesis, the workshop aims to foster interdisciplinary collaboration and help shape the future of scalable, practical, and impactful QML.

title: Graphics4Science 2026: Graphics for Cross-Scale Reliable Scientific Instruments
description: Computer graphics is increasingly central to modern scientific discovery, providing not only visualization but also physically grounded modeling, simulation, inverse problem formulations, and interactive systems for hypothesis generation. Building on the Graphics4Science course at SIGGRAPH 2025, Graphics4Science 2026 adopts a workshop-style format with fewer broadcast talks, deeper technical presentations, and more discussion. The program features four keynotes spanning computational imaging, scientific reconstruction for structural biology, generative modeling for molecular structures, and reduced-order modeling for physical simulation, complemented by two curated lightning-talk sessions highlighting advances from the community. As graphics-based methods move from retrospective analysis toward guiding experiments and engineering decisions, a shared requirement across these areas is reliability. In this workshop, reliable scientific instruments refers to methods and systems that remain useful under scientific constraints, including limited or noisy measurements, distribution shift across instruments and conditions, and the need to communicate uncertainty. This notion of reliability

title: NVIDIA Graphics Developer Tools Roadmap: Agentic AI and Beyond (Second Offering)
description: This presentation offers a focused update on the state of NVIDIA's graphics developer tools and a look at how agentic AI workflows are shaping their future direction. Covering recent advances in Nsight Graphics, Nsight Systems, and Nsight Aftermath, the session highlights key improvements to debugging, profiling, and crash analysis capabilities alongside an exploration of where AI-driven automation is heading next. Attendees will come away with a grounded understanding of the current tooling landscape and practical insight into the agentic AI features being built for graphics developers. This presentation is paired with two hands-on labs where participants will directly use and explore AI-assisted workflows. Presenters Aurelio Reis NVIDIA Jonathan Litt NVIDIA

title: SIGGRAPH 2026 Opening Session & SIGGRAPH 2026 Keynote: Lanny Smoot, Inventions, Innovations, and Imagination: Lanny Smoot’s Prolific Path
description: One of Disney’s most prolific inventors is taking the SIGGRAPH 2026 stage for an exciting presentation that will have attendees reflecting on the power of research, activating emerging technologies, and the value of cross-industry connections to drive technological advancements in one’s career. Lanny Smoot will share some of the significant inventions and innovations he’s made during his long career in research and development, starting by working backward through his 27 years at Walt Disney Imagineering R&D and to his earlier 20-year career at the nation’s telecommunications giants: Bell Labs, Bellcore, SAIC, and Telcordia. Along the way, Lanny will invite us into his lab and will describe the career lessons he’s learned — the importance of both research and new ideas and the necessity for collaboration between people with disparate talents. He will describe how he, and many engineers and scientists, are inspired to come into their fields and how young practitioners can find success. As a long-time contributor to and friend of SIGGRAPH, Lanny will reflect on some of his professional contributions to the conference, including the Emerging Technologies systems he’s dreamed up: “The

title: State of Rigging Tools in the industry
description: We invite animators, riggers, and engineers to discuss rigging tools (third party and proprietary), workflows and technology trends in the industry.

title: Substance by Adobe | The AI Inflection Point: What It Actually Means for the Next Generation of 3D Artists
description: Generative AI hasn't taken the 3D artist out of the picture, but it has changed what the job actually looks like.Generative AI hasn't taken the 3D artist out of the picture, but it has changed what the job actually looks like. The technical steps that used to take years to learn can now be handled or accelerated by AI tools that any student can access today. So what does that mean for how 3D is taught? This panel brings together four people working at the intersection of education, production, and industry to have an honest conversation about where things stand. A university professor, an independent creator who has built a massive self-taught online audience, a CG production veteran, and an Adobe industry strategist will dig into one core question: if AI is handling more of the technical execution, what should educators actually be focusing on? The conversation won't be about which software is best. It's about how different approaches to 3D creation, procedural, generative, real-time, represent genuinely different ways of thinking about the craft, and what that means for students trying to build a career. If you're a student wondering whether your degree is preparing you for the i

title: Towards Co-design in Computer Graphics: Building Collaborations between Researchers and Artists
description: We invite researchers and artists to discuss co-designing computer graphics technologies. Building collaborations between researchers and artists is essential to designing tools for visual production that use novel techniques to support human creative processes rather than supplant or degrade them. Drawing from the organizers’ experience in designing creative software in academic and industry settings, we will discuss concrete methods to build mutually beneficial collaborations with creative practitioners. We see co-design as a way to simultaneously foster scientific contributions and powerful creative workflows for artists, and more broadly as a way to collectively define meaningful directions for innovation in the field.

title: Can AI Judge Its Own Work? Self‑Assessment and Trust in Creative AI Pipelines
description: Generative and agentic AI increasingly grades its own output. VLM and LLM judges decide when a result is good enough, confidence scores gate quality, and autonomous tools self‑terminate. But when can we trust an AI's assessment of its own work? This facilitated discussion gathers researchers and practitioners across graphics, vision, HCI, and production to compare notes on AI self‑assessment and calibration. We will discuss where self‑scores break down, how overconfidence hides in iterative loops, and which external‑validation strategies work (second‑model judges, geometric checks, human review). Bring your own failure cases. Researchers, engineers, and students welcome.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Debugging with Nsight Graphics (Second Offering)
description: Modern graphics applications are getting increasingly difficult to debug. Ray Tracing, advanced rendering pipelines with neural rendering, GPU crashes, and performance bottlenecks often require developers to navigate large amounts of data before identifying the true source of a problem. At the same time, AI-powered tools are beginning to transform how developers investigate and understand complex systems. In this hands-on lab, participants will enter the Hall of Mysteries, an interactive graphics debugging experience built around a series of realistic rendering and performance challenges. Using a preview of the in-development Nsight Graphics MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, participants will study a Vulkan application to investigate visual artifacts, analyze renderer behavior, diagnose GPU crashes, and optimize performance using professional graphics debugging workflows. Rather than following a scripted tutorial or traditional use of the tooling, participants will gather evidence, formulate hypotheses, inspect captures and traces, and verify conclusions through AI-guided investigation. Along the way, they will learn how AI-assisted workflows can accelerate debugging while prese

title: OpenEXR
description: Open discussion with project maintainers about new features and future directions. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: OpenVDB
description: Significant changes in the upcoming major release of OpenVDB. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: USD, Hydra, OpenExec and OpenSubdiv: Pixar’s Open Source Meetup
description: Join the discussion with the developers and users of Universal Scene Description (USD), Hydra, OpenExec, and OpenSubdiv. We'll showcase work from the past year and the plans ahead.

title: Workstations: From the Desk to the Datacenter
description: As creative and technical workflows become more remote, the traditional tower workstation is no longer the only answer. This session explores the spectrum from deskside workstations to rackmount systems, VDI, and cloud-based platforms. We will compare common tools and deployment models, discuss the strengths and tradeoffs of each approach, and examine how the AI boom and memory shortages are changing infrastructure decisions. When does local hardware still win? When does centralized or cloud infrastructure make more sense? Join an open discussion on where workflows are headed and where the long-term equilibrium may settle.

title: Maxon Computer Inc | Preparing Students for Creative Careers: Teaching the Tools and Workflows the Industry Uses
description: Creative talent opens the door, but it is hands-on experience with professional tools and production workflows that prepares students for successful careers. Join Steve Forde, Chief Product Officer at Maxon, as he explores how educators can better align teaching with the realities of today's creative industries. Drawing on examples from film, broadcast, motion graphics, visual effects, and game development, Steve will discuss the technologies and collaborative workflows used by studios around the world, and why experience with industry-standard tools continues to be an important advantage for graduates entering the workforce. The session will also introduce Maxon's growing education initiatives designed to help educators bring students closer to professional practice. These include classroom support from Maxon's Solutions Experts through guest lectures, masterclasses, and curriculum guidance, along with a new skills assessment tool that enables students to measure their progress and build confidence as they develop practical production skills. Rather than focusing on software features alone, this session looks at how educators can bridge the gap between classroom learning and profe

title: Art Gallery
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: SIGGRAPH Thesis Fast Forward
description: The SIGGRAPH Thesis Fast Forward is a forum for Ph.D. students in computer graphics to present and broadcast their research in 3 minutes or less. Students from around the world introduce us to a wide variety of topics spanning research over the last five years.

title: The Last 20%: Getting GenAI to a Deliverable Frame
description: The strongest pipelines pair the speed of AI with the control of traditional workflows, keep artists in command, and hold context as they scale across production, including air-gapped and on-prem. Production with AI is not one shot and a handover. It is continuous loops of iteration: matching color and scene-linear workflows, holding continuity across the frames that carry a story, and moving between AI tools and DCCs until the work is right. Artists, pipeline engineers, and tool builders show how they are building these AI-centric pipelines today, then open the floor: what works, what breaks, and what comes next.

Now, with AI accelerating the research-to-screen pipeline, two exceptional panels bridged by a VES fireside chat rally to seize what comes next. title: NVIDIA: How AI is Changing Education
description: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally reshaping how students learn, how educators teach, and how institutions deliver knowledge at scale. This panel brings together innovators at the forefront of AI-driven education — from Studyfetch, a leading AI-powered study platform transforming how students engage with course material, to NVIDIA's Deep Learning Institute and Community Educator programs, which are equipping the next generation of AI practitioners worldwide. Panelists will explore the ways AI is fundamentally changing how we teach and learn. Generative AI and large language models are personalizing the learning experience, accelerating skill development, and democratizing access to high-quality education. Discussion will cover real-world deployment of AI tools in academic and professional training environments, the challenges of integrating these technologies responsibly, and what the future classroom looks like as AI capabilities continue to advance. Attendees will leave with a practical understanding of how AI is being applied in education today, key considerations for educators and institutions adopting these tools, and a clear perspective on where human instruction and AI

title: SIGGRAPH Asia 2026
description: SIGGRAPH Asia 2026, the 19th ACM SIGGRAPH conference and exhibition on computer graphics and interactive techniques in Asia, will take place from 1 to 4 December 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre KLCC, Malaysia, its first edition in the country. As the sister conference to SIGGRAPH, it travels across Asia, blending innovation with local culture. This session is designed for those new to SIGGRAPH Asia, offering a clear introduction to what the conference is about, how to participate, and what to expect. It also provides an open platform to ask questions, exchange ideas, and connect with the community.

title: Textiles
description: Textiles are everywhere in graphics, from simulation and rendering to fabrication and robotics. Come to this Birds of a Feather to meet others interested in the unique challenges and opportunities textiles present to the graphics community. We'll talk about current state of the art techniques, what problems still need to be solved, and where research is going in the future. Cool demos are encouraged!

title: Flashover: Spatial Storytelling of Wildfire from Memory to Spatial Experience
description: We present Flashover, a spatial storytelling project that reframes wildfire as lived experience: memory, repetition, and loss. We describe how multi-zone installation design and point-based volumetric imagery invite active perception rather than spectacle, translating traumatic memory into embodied encounter and reflection on the climate crisis.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: AI-Assisted Graphics Profiling with Nsight Systems (Second Offering)
description: This training lab offers graphics developers a look at the agentic future being built into Nsight Systems, with a focus on holistic analysis of CPU and GPU performance. Participants will work through hands-on scenarios where AI agents assist with performance analysis . The session bridges the gap between emerging model capabilities and the practical realities of real-time and offline graphics pipelines. Developers at all levels will gain actionable insight into how agentic AI workflows can be adopted today and what innovations are on the horizon. Presenters Dan Binyamin NVIDIA Uri Shomroni NVIDIA

title: Inside DAC: Creative Connections Across SIGGRAPH’s Digital Arts Community
description: Looking for ways to connect with SIGGRAPH’s creative side? This session offers an open and inviting introduction to the ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community (DAC), a vibrant network that brings together artists, designers, technologists, and researchers working across digital and computational media arts. Attendees will get a clear overview of how DAC supports creative exchange through inclusive programs designed to encourage experimentation, dialogue, and collaboration across disciplines. The session highlights key DAC initiatives, including SPARKS Lightning Talks, online and hybrid exhibitions, and the student digital art competition, along with opportunities for global engagement through partnerships with organizations such as ISEA and Expanded Animation. Whether you are a student, a first-time SIGGRAPH attendee, or someone curious about engaging more deeply with SIGGRAPH’s creative communities, this session provides a friendly entry point into DAC’s activities and a chance to discover how you can share your work, connect with others, and participate in an international network of digital arts practitioners.

title: ASWF Diversity & Inclusion Working Group
description: The Diversity & Inclusion WG at the Academy Software Foundation breaks down barriers to access and increases connections among participants in the Foundation’s projects, community, and the VFX & Animation industry. If you are passionate about the Entertainment industry and looking for a way to get involved, please consider attending! Our group members consist of engineers, students, faculty, and more. We'll provide group updates as well as save time for community discussion. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage
description: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage is an interactive, generative art installation that re-imagines Los Angeles as a living, ever-evolving digital twin. Conceived for SIGGRAPH 2026, the project bridges LA’s rich cinematic legacy with the frontier of spatial computing. By seamlessly integrating Kinect motion tracking and AI-generated point clouds, it invites participants to co-author a collaborative digital monument. As users input their personal memories and desires of LA, individual landmarks dynamically merge, overlap, and fold into a singular, massive 3D cyber environment—blurring the boundaries between physical presence, collective memory, and cybernetic space. Credit： Visual & Interactive Director: Jimmz ZHANG, Digital FUN & TEA Community Technical Director: Ruokun CHEN, TEA Community & FLARE Lab Sound Artist: Louzhang, USC School of Cinematic Arts

title: Open Review Initiative, including OpenRV and xSTUDIO
description: Updates from the Open Review Initiative team. Demonstration of the latest work in live collaboration protocols including support for annotations. Progress and community updates from OpenRV and xSTUDIO, as well as our work on Encoding, and Plugin Development. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: Poster Sessions
description: New ideas start here. Join us to hear from authors as they discuss their work.

title: The Geometry Gap: Ground-Truth 4D Data for World Models and Physical AI
description: World models and robot policies learn physical dynamics mostly from monocular video and simulation — both geometrically underdetermined, so models approximate depth and structure rather than measure it. When those predictions drive a robot, hallucinated geometry becomes a safety problem. This session convenes graphics, vision, robotics, and world-model researchers to discuss the missing substrate: ground-truth 4D capture (calibrated multi-view capture, 3D/4D Gaussian splatting, and multi-sensor fusion) and how to scale it. We'll discuss and debate where real spatiotemporal ground truth comes from, real2sim versus sim2real efficacy, implicit versus explicit, and what's next for world models.

title: UNC Computer Science SIGGRAPH Alumni Lunch
description: UNC Department of Computer Science SIGGRAPH Reunion Luncheon By invitation only. To receive an invitation email: er@cs.unc.edu Hosted by Henry Fuchs and Praneeth Chakravarthula.

title: Designing Interfaces for Aphantasia
description: Aphantasia affects how people use interfaces that assume visual memory, mental imagery, or the ability to “picture” a previous state. This Birds of a Feather session will discuss UI design patterns that make software more accessible for people who do not visualize internally: persistent previews, visible history, explicit labels, side-by-side comparisons, concrete examples, saved states, clearer spatial cues, and reduced reliance on memory-based workflows. The goal is to identify practical design guidelines for creative tools, AI tools, visualization software, and everyday applications.

title: Behind the Scenes at The New York Times R&D: Spatial Storytelling
description: "The New York Times has an R&D team?" In our industry dedicated R&D is rare. We make space for technologists and journalists to pilot new ways of reporting stories. Learn how we navigate journalistic constraints, pioneer spatial storytelling techniques, and bridge gaps between emerging technology and traditional journalism workflows.

title: DAC: SPARKS in Review: Digital Art and SE Asia, Performance, Immateriality, Interabilities, Artificial Life, AI in Filmmaking, and Animism
description: This session will present the highlights from the fall 2025 and spring 2026 SPARKS (Short Presentations for the Kindred Spirit) sessions.

title: Epic Games | Preparing Students for Real-Time: The Hidden Production Shift in 3D Animation
description: The animation industry is shifting, and real-time technology is at the center. But the biggest change isn't purely technical; it's how teams collaborate, make decisions, and move from concept to final shot. This session draws on real studio case studies to reveal the production mindset behind real-time animation in Unreal Engine: collapsed silos, new collaborative workflows, and what that means for classroom learning, curriculum, and the skills students need to succeed. Contributor Jamie Hurcomb Epic Games

title: Khronos Fast Forward
description: If you build graphics, XR, 3D, compute, or AI applications, the Khronos Group is helping shape the open standards behind the tools, engines, devices, and platforms you use every day. Kick off your SIGGRAPH week with Khronos Fast Forward, a rapid fire look at the latest advances in Vulkan, Slang, OpenXR, glTF, ANARI, WebGL, machine learning, and AI. Discover what is new, what is next, and where to dive deeper throughout the week through BOFs, talks, demos, and technical discussions.

title: OpenTimelineIO
description: OpenTimelineIO is an interchange format and API for editorial timeline information. Please join us for an overview of what OpenTimelineIO does and some updates from the past year. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: MaterialX and OpenPBR
description: Join MaterialX and OpenPBR Technical Steering Committee members and contributors as they share recent milestones, future plans and answer questions from the community. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: From Perception to Prediction - Developing Physical AI with Cosmos 3
description: In this lab, we'll work hands-on across three core capabilities of NVIDIA Cosmos 3: reasoning, world generation, and action prediction. We'll use Cosmos 3 Nano Reasoner to caption real robot video, generate augmentation prompts, and validate synthetic footage using chain-of-thought reasoning. We'll then use Cosmos 3 and Cosmos-Transfer2.5 to synthesize and transform photorealistic video from those prompts, scaling training data without new recording sessions. Finally, we'll apply Cosmos 3 in different action modes to translate scene understanding into embodied control commands, completing the perceive → reason → act loop.

title: Hands-on Course: Dreaming in 4 dimensions: generating media with Gemini, Genie, and Veo
description: Move beyond static pixels into explorable, playable worlds, generative images, and generative video. Join Google DeepMind engineers as we get hands-on, using NanoBanana Pro for reasoned concept art, Veo 3.1 for video generation, and Genie 3 to turn images and text into playable, interactive worlds.

title: SideFX | Learning Matters. Certification Proves It.
description: As the demand for skilled Houdini artists continues to grow across the visual effects, animation, and gaming industries, education programs face increasing pressure to prepare students with both technical expertise and industry-recognized credentials. This session will explore the value of certification at every level of the learning journey, including School, Instructor, and Artist Certifications, and how these programs help establish educational quality, validate skills, and create clearer pathways from the classroom to industry careers. Attendees will gain insight into how certification benefits students, instructors, and institutions by providing measurable standards of achievement and helping align training with industry expectations. We will discuss the role certifications play in building confidence, enhancing employability, and supporting educational excellence. The session will also highlight the extensive resources available to support educators teaching Houdini. These include free instructor licenses, curriculum reviews, a dedicated learning management system, and an instructor community designed to foster collaboration and knowledge sharing. In addition, we will showcas

title: ACES
description: Join ACES Technical Steering Committee members and fellow imaging professionals for an open, forward-looking discussion on the digital color workflows. Bring your feedback, challenges, and ideas as we discuss current feature support, cross-application interoperability, and what ACES should tackle next. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

title: Building with Meta Wearable Device Access Toolkit
description: Create at the forefront of spatial computing with Meta’s Wearable Device Access Toolkit. This immersive, 3-hour course offers hands-on access to our AI Glasses hardware and sensor data, empowering attendees to build privacy-aware, innovative AI Glasses applications. Participants will advance wearable technology setting new standards for responsible, visionary digital experiences.

title: Computer Graphics History Institute
description: The Computer Graphics History Institute (CGHI) is building a knowledgebase of computer graphics history. It has a twofold mission: a repository about the history of computer graphics, for education and reference, and technology bases for research into several areas, including HCI. As a repository, the goal is to provide a starting point to learn about the history of computer graphics. The knowledgebase is an RDF graph database. Research areas include history as KM, KM with XR, and interfaces based on cognitive science, where the primary interaction is an API to the human cognitive system. There will be demos.

title: Controlling the Frame: AI VFX, Generative Video, and the Future of Compositing
description: AI is rapidly reshaping VFX workflows, enabling tasks like relighting, background transformation, and look development to happen after capture. But as these systems become more capable, a central question emerges: how much control do artists actually retain? This BOF explores the rise of controllable AI VFX and hybrid compositing workflows, where traditional techniques intersect with learned systems. Drawing on emerging production practices, we invite artists, technologists, and researchers to discuss where these approaches succeed, where they break down, and how workflows are evolving across lighting, consistency, and creative intent.

title: Differentiable Physics for Graphics and AI
description: Differentiable Physics for Graphics and AI (DPGA) is a half-day SIGGRAPH technical workshop exploring how differentiable physics-based simulation is transforming graphics and related fields including robotics, 3D vision, design, and fabrication. As simulation becomes tightly integrated with optimization, learning, and generative pipelines, differentiability enables new workflows for inverse problems, system identification, and physics-aware design. The workshop is organized by researchers from Carnegie Mellon University, UCLA, the University of Utah, and Meta Reality Labs, whose work spans physical simulation, graphics systems, robotics, and AI. Together with invited speakers from academia and industry, they bring perspectives from foundational simulation research to large-scale production systems and real-world applications. The workshop brings together foundational methods, practical systems, and emerging applications in differentiable simulation across graphics, robotics, 3D vision, design, and fabrication. Through keynote talks, lightning presentations, and a moderated panel, it will showcase both what differentiable physics can already enable and the technical barriers that st

title: Faces & Avatars
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Frontiers Workshop: CTRL-G: Controllable Generative Graphics for Games
description: The CTRL-G (Controllable Generative Graphics for Games) workshop explores the critical intersection of generative AI and interactive game development. While generative models are transforming content creation, the central challenge for games remains control: steering these models in real time to align with player intent, production pipelines, and artistic requirements. This three-hour session brings together AI researchers, graphics/VFX professionals, and ethicists to address two core themes: developing controllable world models—such as adaptive, player-responsive environments—and evolving game engine education to incorporate these new hybrid workflows. By bridging technical innovation with production realities and ethical considerations, CTRL-G aims to define a practical agenda for integrating generative AI into modern game engines. Participants will gain actionable insights into state-of-the-art techniques, integration challenges, and the pedagogical shifts necessary to prepare the next generation of developers for an AI-augmented industry.

title: GFXReconstruct - an Engine for Vulkan Debugging and Profiling
description: GFXReconstruct is an open-source framework for capturing, replaying, and analyzing Vulkan applications. This BOF will explore how developers can use GFXReconstruct as the foundation for debugging, profiling, and performance analysis workflows, both from the command line and through integrated tools. Attendees will learn about recent project updates, use cases, and opportunities to contribute feedback and help guide future development.

title: OUT OF THE ASHES: A Work-In-Progress Spatial Story of the Human Cost of Climate Change
description: OUT OF THE ASHES co-directors Nonny de la Peña and Rory Mitchell detail the process of creating the work-in-progress immersive documentary beginning with 3D scanning the devastation in the aftermath of the Los Angeles Fires of 2025 through the conception and execution of 3DGS & 4DGS pipelines for Unity.

description: Join top female Animation/VFX leaders for a candid discussion on navigating technical career paths, overcoming obstacles, and building resilience. This annual Birds of a Feather offers actionable strategies and future perspectives on women in technology and leadership. The panel runs 2-3pm, followed by a networking reception. Panelists: Munira Tayabji, Chief Technology Officer, Walt Disney Animation Studios Rachel Rose, Director of R&D, ILM Cindy Eng, VP Studio Pipeline & Platform, DreamWorks Animation Jacqueline Aleson-O'Farrell, Technology Engagement and Development, Laika Moderator: Sarah Counnas, Sr. Director, Technology Communication & Strategy, DreamWorks Animation

title: Best of Eurographics 2026
description: Following the success of last year’s edition, this session will present a curated selection of outstanding research contributions from the Eurographics 2026 conference. The session aims to provide SIGGRAPH attendees with insights into current trends, emerging directions, and key developments in Computer Graphics and related fields, with a particular focus on research originating from the European community and its global impact. The session will open with a short introduction to Eurographics, followed by presentations of the selected works and awards, in particular: - The Best Paper Award of Eurographics 2026 - The Selected Honourable Mentions of the Best Paper Award of Eurographics 2026 - The recipient of the Eurographics Young Researcher Award 2026

title: Foundry | Shaping Future Storytellers: Integrating Advanced 3D, AI and VP Workflows
description: To prepare students for modern workflows, educators must teach the core concepts behind the craft, not just the technical steps to finish a task. Juan Salazar, Foundry’s Creative Director, will review the latest advancements in Nuke’s 3D system, Nuke Stage, and Griptape. Plus, he will discuss how these tools fit into a broader, future-facing vision for AI and virtual production, offering advice on how to train artists who understand how to truly evaluate and direct their tools. Contributor Juan Salazar Foundry

title: The State of Creative Coding: Processing Foundation Community Meetup
description: For over two decades, Processing Java and p5.js have provided a learning environment and a technical substrate for artists, educators, and developers to create generative art and interaction design through code. This session brings the community together to discuss where the Processing ecosystem stands today — and where it's headed. Processing Foundation will share 25–26 technical milestones and 2D and 3D demonstrations, followed by an overview of the R&D work currently underway to pave the path for Processing 5. We'll leave ample time for participants to share their work and contribute to the projects through feedback, questions, and discussion.

title: Wētā FX Presents: Avatar: Fire and Ash
description: In Avatar: Fire and Ash, we return to Pandora’s environments and biomes, creatures and clans, and immerse audiences in water, and dazzle them with fire. The Academy Award winning VFX team from Wētā FX and Lightstorm Entertainment will overview the process of creating the film’s 3,100+ shots including how the teams blended extensive native stereo photography with CG environments and fully digital performance-captured characters. While advanced tools are required to work at such scale, everything was handcrafted - every character, tree, leaf, pore, sweat drop and insect – to ensure Pandora is a believable world. To open the session, Nicky, and Peter, will outline the logistical challenges Fire and Ash presented: managing, scheduling, and supporting a team of four Visual Effects Supervisors, six Sequence VFX Supervisors, two Animation Supervisors, and more than 1,200 crew over the course of roughly three years. sharing learnings of how 1 and 2 helped chart a smoother path. Eric will discuss how the film is the product of 20 years of close collaboration and technical achievements. Wētā FX and Lightstorm entertainment, have worked on Avatar projects for the last 20 years, establishing a

title: 3DGS in Web Gaming: ‘The Shadow of the Czar’
description: ‘The Shadow of the Czar’ is the jazz-infused, Webby-honored noir game by James C. Kane featuring breakthrough use of 3D gaussian splat generation, reconstruction and rendering techniques in the browser. This demo will show how the hit cross-platform web title was developed solo and open sourced in just six weeks. Contributor James Kane jamesckane.io

description: Presentations and open discussion about integrating OpenUSD and MaterialX in production pipelines, with successes and failures, possible how-tos. This BoF is part of a full day of open source BoFs hosted by the Academy Software Foundation as part of Open Source Days. Learn more at opensourcedays.aswf.io.

description: We will be gathering again to discuss the following: * What are the most important features of smartglasses that have appeared in the last couple years? * Which glasses are reportedly making good use of AI? Can any currently do XR or SLAM? * What would be the killer app for glasses? After this, we shall break up in groups to allow participants to try on as many different kind of glasses as we can find for this session. If you have a pair and are willing to demo it to others, please bring it with you and email me: johnson@wmich.edu

To ensure a safe, respectful environment, the ACM Code of Conduct will be strictly enforced. Harassment or non-consensual behavior, including unauthorized photography, will not be tolerated. Come help build community, find support, and learn about the future of the Rainbow Affinity Group.

title: SIGGRAPH Roundtables: Beyond the Talk
description: Engage. Reflect. Connect. For its second year, SIGGRAPH 2026 hosts the Roundtables — a series of intimate, speaker-led discussions that carry the conversation beyond the stage. Each 90-minute roundtable seats up to 10 participants in a focused, informal setting. Every table centers on a specific topic drawn from a SIGGRAPH 2026 Talk and is led by the speaker who presented it — a chance to share experiences, ask the questions there's never time for after a Talk, and explore emerging ideas alongside peers and contributors from across disciplines. Seating is first-come, first-served, so we encourage you to arrive early to join the conversation that resonates most. Session Format: Each roundtable follows a simple, engaging structure: * Opening reflection (3–5 minutes) * Participant introductions (10 minutes) * Group discussion (30 minutes) * Optional table transition (10 minutes) * Stay or explore another topic * Continued discussion (25–30 minutes) * Closing summary (10 minutes) Roundtable Themes: Table 1: Engine Room - Real-time VFX & Virtual Production Pipelines, Unreal Engine + OpenUSD with He Sun and Tzung-da Tsai Table 2: Truth in the Age of AI: Governance, Provenance, and the Fu

title: Sponsored Keynote by NVIDIA: Jan Kautz, Ming-Yu Liu, and Edward Liu; Next Era of Graphics — Neural Rendering, World Models, and Simulation
description: Join NVIDIA Research and Engineering leaders to experience the latest advances shaping computer graphics and simulation. We will explore breakthrough neural rendering techniques, progress in developing world models, and simulation methods for AI, built by AI. Through recent research examples and demos, we’ll look at how these technologies are changing the way digital worlds are built, rendered, and used across every domain — creative tools, industrial design, robotics, and autonomous systems.

At SIGGRAPH 2026, the Immersive Pavilion has even more contributed pieces with elements of human sensation or interaction than years previous. Explore how the ever-changing landscapes of XR technology allow for more advanced human-centered design at this panel discussion with our esteemed contributors from this year's Immersive Pavilion.
