title: Visualizing the Moon for Artemis II
description: Artemis II is the first crewed spaceflight to the Moon, in fact the first beyond Earth orbit, since Apollo 17 in 1972. The four astronauts will fly around the far side of the Moon on the sixth day of their mission, and for a six-hour period during the flyby, they will be observing, photographing, and describing the geology of the side of the Moon never seen from Earth, including areas never seen by human eyes in daylight even by Apollo astronauts. Cinematic scientific visualization using high-end commercial 3D rendering software has played a crucial role in both planning the observations and in training the astronauts. This kind of visualization combines rendering realism with large NASA datasets to create highly accurate representations of the lighting conditions the astronauts are likely to encounter. Small differences in Sun angle can reveal or hide topographic, albedo, and color features of the greatest interest to lunar scientists. For this reason, the final observation plan won’t be created until after the launch, when the precise trajectory and lunar arrival time are known. A six-hour realtime visualization of the flyby will be rendered from Mission Control on day 2 of the f

We plan to invite people working on fabrication widely using our own connections, but also welcome anybody interested in this topic. This will be the 5th “Bring Your Bunny (or Something)” BoF event with previous events taking place at SIGGRAPH in 2023, 2024, and 2025, and SIGGRAPH Asia in 2024.

title: Countering Racial Bias in Computer Graphics Research: Five Years Later
description: This BOF is for attendees interested in discussing racial bias and related issues embedded in computer graphics research. It is a follow-on to similarly titled BOFs at SIGGRAPH 2021, 2022, 2024, and 2025. We will celebrate progress over the last five years, discuss setbacks, and brainstorm paths forward. This will be a friendly, collaborative space for mutual, authentic engagement across difference. Attendees can be at any stage, including learning about, taking steps toward, or enacting change in computer graphics research.

title: Electronic Theater (separate ticket required)
description: Every year the Electronic Theater highlights a selection of the very best in computer animation and this year's edition continues in this tradition. The prestigious, Oscar® qualifying festival is your ticket to this best, and soon to be buzziest, short film projects. Opening night also includes the presentation of the festival awards!

title: Chapters Party
description: Celebrate the start of SIGGRAPH with an evening you won't want to miss! Join the ACM SIGGRAPH Chapters Committee from 9 pm to 1 am on Monday, July 20 at Exchange LA for a night of fun with the world’s greatest pixel wranglers. All registration levels are welcome, and your conference badge is your entry ticket! You must be of legal drinking age to attend and provide a valid photo ID in order to enter the club. The venue is a 10 minute car ride away from the Los Angeles Convention Center at 618 S Spring Street, Los Angeles, CA. Ask for more information at the ACM SIGGRAPH Village!

title: Pathfinders
description: FIRST-TIME ATTENDEE RESOURCE: Pathfinders will be available to help you find your way. Pathfinders is a volunteer group dedicated to mentoring first-time conference visitors. The Pathfinders team can offer detailed explanations and descriptions of all SIGGRAPH programs or venues, and help you build a schedule based on your personal skills and interests.

title: From Splats to Worlds: Advanced 3DGS Applications
description: "Gaussian splatting has rapidly evolved from a novel radiance field reconstruction technique into a versatile foundation for real-time rendering, volumetric media, and spatial computing. This BoF session brings together researchers, engineers, and industry practitioners to explore the progression from 3D to 4D Gaussian representation. Discussions will cover compression; animated Gaussian splatting; volumetric media from capture through distribution; 3DGS as a building block for world models bridging neural scene understanding with real-time engines; and the potential for 3DGS as a real-world archival format. Come share progress, surface interoperability challenges, and help align where the ecosystem is heading.

title: 3D Jobs Outside of Entertainment - A Panel by The 3D Artist Community
description: Ever thought about taking your 3D skills beyond entertainment but had no idea where to start? Join a panel of working 3D artists who've built careers outside of VFX, gaming, and animation. They'll share how they made the leap into industries like fashion, product visualization, automotive design, motion graphics, and more. Whether you're curious about a pivot or just want to know what's out there, this conversation will open your eyes to how far your skills can take you. Come with questions!

title: Advances in Real-time Rendering in Games, Part I
description: Modern video games employ a variety of sophisticated algorithms to produce groundbreaking 3D rendering pushing the visual boundaries and interactive experience of rich environments. This course brings state-of-the-art and production-proven rendering techniques for fast, interactive rendering of complex and engaging virtual worlds of video games. This is the course to attend if you are in the game development industry or want to learn the latest and greatest techniques in the real-time rendering domain!

title: Human–AI Co-Creation in Generative Art: Graphics Methods, Systems, and Applications
description: Recent advances in generative AI are transforming how visual and spatial content is created, enabling new forms of collaboration between humans and intelligent systems. This workshop explores human–AI co-creation in generative art, where AI acts not merely as an automation tool but as a creative partner supporting exploration, iteration, and artistic expression. The workshop brings together researchers, artists, and engineers from computer graphics, machine learning, human–computer interaction, and computational design to examine emerging systems for collaborative creativity. Topics include interactive creative tools, generative models for visual and 3D art, and fabrication-aware design systems that connect digital creation with real-world production. The workshop will feature keynote talks and interactive discussions exploring questions such as artistic control in generative systems, effective interfaces for co-creation, and integrating fabrication constraints into generative design, aiming to inspire new directions for human–AI collaboration in generative art and design. More details in: https://human-ai-cocreation.github.io/

title: Hydra in Production
description: Based on real production examples, Hydra in Production will present best practices and case studies of how to integrate OpenUSD's Hydra library into a content creation tool or pipeline, and leverage it to solve complex pipeline challenges and enable powerful workflows. This is a companion course to USD in Production.

description: Join us for a relaxed and informative mingling event designed for those interested in exploring volunteering opportunities within the ACM SIGGRAPH organisation and in SIGGRAPH Conferences. This is a great chance to meet current key volunteers and leadership, learn about the various roles and committees that you could get involved in, and discover how your skills and passions could make a meaningful impact to our community. Whether you're looking to expand your professional network, gain new experiences, or give back to the community, this event provides a valuable opportunity to get involved with SIGGRAPH!

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | World Models : The Frontier of Physical AI, Unlocked By Graphics
description: Physical AI begins with the ability to see, understand, generate, and simulate the world. In this opening address, Ming-Yu Liu will explore why world models are becoming one of AI’s most exciting frontiers, and why 3D graphics is central to the shift. The session will dive into the latest open frontier world model, NVIDIA Cosmos 3, exploring its breakthrough architecture built to accelerate vision reasoning, synthetic data generation, and policy training. Attendees will also learn how generative AI, simulation technologies, and agentic AI are converging to advance physical AI.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Animating Creatures of the Upside Down with Wētā FX: The Creepy Realism of Stranger Things Season 5
description: In this talk, Dave Clayton, five-time Academy and BAFTA awards-nominated Animation Supervisor at Wētā FX, will break down some of the techniques used to imbue the creatures and characters of Stranger Things with a new level of creepy realism. Coming on board for the iconic show’s final season, Wētā FX contributed more than 1,100 shots across all eight episodes, for which they received 4 VES nominations. Starting with Vecna, Dave will review how the team built the villain on concept art from the client, and extended his look and build from prior seasons. From his deadly vines coiling and extending from within his body, to the surprising methods used to arrive at Vecna's ‘charred’ makeover. He’ll also explain the process of bringing the Demogorgons and Demodogs to new scary-real creative heights though the use of mocap, rag-doll dynamics, muscle simulations and keyframe animation — making them feel a natural part of the Stranger Things creature menagerie. Finally he’ll dive into the team’s work on the ever-looming Mindflayer: how they animated continuous action to help bring the grand battle finale to a pulse-pounding climax, while crafting its motion to reinforce its scale, weight,

title: Chasing De’Snake! - Developing the Worlds and Characters of Disney’s “Zootopia 2”
description: When building upon a beloved franchise like Zootopia, the artists of Walt Disney Animation Studios had to consider every detail that makes the world unique and its characters memorable. By combining the famous bunny and fox duo with a herd of new animated animals and environments, artists developed new rules and techniques to expand the world in genuine and exciting ways. In this session, the “Zootopia 2” leadership team will take you on a wild journey through the film’s worldbuilding. They will explore what it took to visualize marshes, mountains, deserts, and tundra, and discuss the development of new characters like Gary De’Snake and the Lynxley family. They will also share new workflows and tools throughout the discussion that allowed for collaboration across multiple departments, and enabled the crew to tackle an adventurous and expansive body of work. Presenters Jack Fulmer Head of Environments Walt Disney Animation Studios Adam Green Animation Supervisor Walt Disney Animation Studios Kira Lehtomaki Head of Animation Walt Disney Animation Studios David Hutchins Head of Effects Animation Walt Disney Animation Studios Tyler Kupferer Director of Cinematography Layout Walt Disney

title: 3D Assets: New features, Tooling, and What Comes Next
description: Interoperable assets are foundational to spatial computing, whether it be representing people, places, or things. This BoF takes a look at some of the interoperable asset work that's gone on between various SDOs in the last year; their ratification status, real-world implementations, available tooling, and sample assets. These are standards that are continuing their development into the next year; this is your opportunity to shape the directions of the standards.

title: 3D challenges in .MIL
description: This is an informal meet-up for Gov/DoD/Mil artists and storytellers to discuss common challenges in our unique work environments. The target audience is individuals with .mil, .gov email addresses.

title: Art Gallery
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: Chaos | Ray-Traced Gaussian Splats in Production: The June July Pipeline
description: Building on the VidViz workflow introduced at SIGGRAPH 2025, this talk documents the next phase of the June July virtual production pipeline. We describe how VidViz planning tools informed the live-action shoot, and how captured location data was transformed into an HDR Gaussian splat using a combination of the XGrids scanner and the Arri Alexa 35. We detail the process of integrating that asset into Chaos Arena and the advantages of working with ray-traced splats in a flexible, real-time stage environment, including lighting evaluation, camera planning, and interactive iteration before a single frame of principal photography is committed.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | OpenUSD for AI Agents, Model-Driven Workflows, and 3D Graphics Pipelines
description: OpenUSD is becoming a foundation for how developers build, connect, and validate 3D graphics pipelines for physical AI. As AI agents and open models begin helping teams create simulation environments, developers need scene data they can direct, inspect, and trust. This session covers how the latest OpenUSD standard work supports modular, interoperable 3D worlds, and how NVIDIA is using OpenUSD across agentic physical AI workflows.

title: Pioneers Featured Speaker - Don Greenberg
description: Computer Graphics legend Don Greenberg is the ACM/SIGGRAPH Pioneers Featured Speaker for 2026! Greenberg's astonishingly multi-disciplinary career started in architecture, and has spanned the computer graphics revolution from its genesis in the 1960s. He has taught in the fields of architecture, engineering and computer science, while also being an elite athlete and life-long advisor to the tennis and soccer teams at Cornell. Greenberg’s teaching has covered computer-aided design in architecture as well as computer animation in the Department of Art, and he has simultaneously taught courses in computer science and technology strategy. Greenberg received the 1987 ACM SIGGRAPH Steven A. Coons Award for Outstanding Creative Contributions to Computer Graphics, its highest honor, and the 1989 NCGA Academic Award, the highest educational award given by the National Computer Graphics Association. He is a member of the National Academy of Engineering; Founding Fellow, American Institute of Medical and Biological Engineering; Fellow, Association for Computer Machinery; Fellow, American Association for the Advancement of Science; and the founding director of the 11-year National Science and

title: Renderfarming: This is an annual discussion of VFX & Animation Large-Scale Rendering, On-prem and Hybrid Farm

With render farm infrastructure, both on-premise and hybrid cloud solutions have software, queue management, reliability, and performance woes. Industry experts and new entrants are all welcomed, for a sharing of knowledge, experience, and best practices. Mail joe@jdamato.com for any questions or comments. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the VFX Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud Native, Pipeline, Studio Storage, and Agentic Workflows

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: Introduction to Slang: The Next-Generation Shading Language
description: This hands-on course introduces Slang, an open-source, open governance shading language hosted by Khronos that simplifies graphics development across platforms. Designed to tackle the growing complexity of shader code, Slang offers modern programming constructs while maintaining top performance on current GPUs.

title: Inside the Show: A Live VR Performance and the Backstage Systems Behind It
description: A live Spatial Storytelling performance that pairs a short VR scene with a real-time “backstage reveal.” One performer acts in VR while a company member projects the live experience onto the screen, then demonstrates the hidden tools that drive the show, including triggers, actor menus, and control systems.

title: Nebius | World Models: Where Games, VFX, and AI Collide
description: World models can already simulate environments in real time, making them playable, editable, and generated on demand. Are they the next game engine, the future of VFX, or a new medium that encompasses both? Join the COO of World Labs, NVIDIA's creative technology lead, and the Head Of AI Media at Nebius for a straight-talk look at what's working now, what's still hype, and what studios and toolmakers need to prepare for.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Building SimReady 3D Worlds for Physical AI
description: Physical AI needs more than realistic 3D graphics. Developers need simulation-ready 3D worlds with the semantics, scale, materials, physics, and validation required for robotics, AI training, and world model development. This session will explore how teams can use SimReady and OpenUSD-based assets to prepare 3D worlds for downstream physical AI workflows. Presenters Jonathan Stephens Lightwheel Steven Ren Palatial

title: Autodesk Vision Series | How to give soul to a faceless alien, question? Animating Rocky in Project Hail Mary with Framestore
description: At the core of Project Hail Mary is an unlikely friendship between an astronaut, Grace, and a faceless alien, Rocky. In this presentation, VFX Supervisor Bob Winter and Animation Supervisor Aulo Licinio break down Framestore’s collaboration with directors Phil Lord and Chris Miller to deliver the emotional heartbeat of the story. Bringing Rocky to life meant inventing a completely unique physical language for a creature with no eyes, no face, and no human expressions. The team will detail how they navigated the unique challenges of controlling a five-legged anatomy, turning strict biological constraints into endearing character quirks. Discover how Framestore blended cutting-edge keyframe animation with physical on-set puppetry to build a tactile, believable connection between the two leads, and see if you can spot where the puppet ends and the digital animation begins. Presenters Bob Winter Framestore Aulo Licinio Framestore

title: Chasing De’Snake! - Developing the Worlds and Characters of Disney’s “Zootopia 2” - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: In Conversation with Brad Bird
description: Join two-time Academy Award-winning filmmaker Brad Bird (“The Incredibles,” “Ratatouille”, “The Iron Giant”) for an exclusive SIGGRAPH conversation about “RAY GUNN,” his highly anticipated animated detective, neo-noir coming to Netflix. Set in Metropia—a dazzling retro-futuristic metropolis imagined as the future through the lens of 1939—RAY GUNN follows Raymond Gunn, the last human private detective, as he becomes entangled in a mystery involving aliens, murder, and a celebrity whose fame may conceal dangerous secrets. Described by Bird as “”The Maltese Falcon” meets “Buck Rogers,”” the film blends classic detective storytelling with expansive science-fiction world-building. For decades, Bird has pushed the boundaries of what animation can achieve, creating richly realized worlds that are as emotionally engaging as they are visually groundbreaking. With “RAY GUNN,” he brings that signature style to an ambitious new universe, combining noir atmosphere, Art Deco-inspired design, and cutting-edge animation to create a world unlike anything seen on screen before. Featuring never-before-seen insights into “RAY GUNN”'s development and production, this session offers a rare opportunity t

title: Materials & Textures
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Phased & Viscous Fluiids

title: ANARI in Action: Open Rendering for Visualization, Simulation, and Discovery
description: ANARI is helping make advanced rendering more accessible by decoupling scene description from rendering implementation, enabling developers to take advantage of modern visualization technologies without deep graphics expertise. Join the Khronos Group and the ANARI community for an interactive look at the latest advances in ANARI, including new capabilities, tooling, real world adoption, and integration across scientific visualization, simulation, digital twins, HPC, and industrial workflows. Bring your questions, share your experiences, and help shape the future of open, scalable rendering.

title: Going Cloud Native
description: Join a participant-driven discussion with key representatives from the graphics community, comparing experiences and exploring techniques related to pushing the production pipeline and resources toward cloud native architecture and deployment. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud, Pipeline, and Storage. Attendees will receive invites to our 'Beers of a Feather' event, the same evening.

title: The Continuous Production Layer: When Do Previs, Techvis, and Postvis Just Become Viz?
description: Previs, techvis, and postvis have traditionally described separate production moments: planning the shot, engineering the shoot, and supporting editorial or VFX after photography. But real-time engines, virtual production, performance capture, simulation, asset-aware workflows, and generative AI are making visualization more continuous across development, production, and post. This Birds of a Feather asks whether visualization is becoming a persistent production layer: carrying creative intent, technical constraints, shot truth, iteration, and review across the pipeline. Participants will discuss what should carry forward, what should stay flexible, and how visualization can remain controllable, traceable, and artist-led.

title: Tool UX Design & Workflow: From Tool Pain to Workflow Gain
description: What features does a material editor need? How do we make a good warning dialog? Why do we need better translate gizmos? How do we get our internal users to use the new tool instead of sticking to the old tool? Ask these questions, and any others you can think of, in this sessions. We will discuss tool UX design & workflow questions that anyone in the room brings up, find solutions and experiences as a group, and find the best answers that allow you to move forward and make the best tools possible for 3D content creation.

title: What's the latest at Chaos? Realtime, world-building, Gaussian splats and artist-friendly AI meet together
description: In this talk we will explore the latest additions to the Chaos M&E products including V-Ray for Houdini, Maya, 3ds Max and Blender, as well as Vantage and Arena.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - LightOpt: Lights Optimization for Real-time Rendering
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: LightOpt: Lights Optimization for Real-time Rendering LightOpt is a differentiable optimization framework that automatically reduces the number of lights in real-time game scenes while preserving visual appearance. By optimizing and restructuring real-time light sources, it lowers lighting cost, reduces overlap, and improves cross-platform performance for modern game rendering. Paper Presentation Presented: Monday, July 20, 3:45-5:15 pm; Paper Session: Real-Time Rendering; Room 408 A

title: Visual Instruments for Live Cinema: Turning Process into Performance
description: Virtual production pipelines (LED volumes, camera tracking, motion capture, and game engines) are deeply interactive systems, yet the final render often conceals the labor and live decision-making behind them. Part audiovisual performance and part artist talk, this session imagines the production process as a live cinema performance, using custom visual instruments to make calibration, mapping, and real-time control visible on stage. Contributor Kevin Peter He Kevin Peter He

title: Faceform | NanoSkin: Human Skin Super-Resolution and Material Acquisition for Photorealistic Digital Doubles at Scale
description: We will present NanoSkin, our approach for increasing the resolution of actor-specific skin microdetail by a factor of 4 to 16. NanoSkin is a neural network trained on our proprietary skin dataset to generate displacement textures up to 16K. Skin microdetail is one of the key factors that contributes to the realism of digital characters. However, one of the most common production approaches is still to use pre-made skin detail maps captured from other people. We will give a high-level overview of the approach we use to enhance the details of a specific actor, rather than inventing them from scratch or transferring them from another person. We will also discuss our work on lookdev automation and automatic material map acquisition, where texture maps are estimated in a way that allows the rendered character to match the actor’s reference photographs as closely as possible. Presenters Andrei Krovopuskov Faceform Kirill Klimov Faceform

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | How to Develop a Real-Time 3D Digital Twin for Rapid Engineering Design
description: Learn how to build a real-time 3D digital twin for rapid engineering design using NVIDIA Omniverse and CUDA-X libraries, AI physics models, and agentic engineering tools. This session will explore how leading simulation providers are leveraging NVIDIA technologies to enable real-time digital twins that accelerate design iteration and improve engineering workflows. We will highlight how Rivian is leveraging Siemens technologies accelerated by NVIDIA and AI physics to achieve rapid automotive design.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Cartographic Visualization Birds of a Feather
description: The ACM SIGGRAPH Cartographic Visualization (Carto) session explores how viewpoints and techniques from the computer graphics community can be effectively applied to cartographic and spatial data sets.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | What's Next for Animation at Autodesk
description: Character animation workflows are evolving rapidly, creating new opportunities for artists to create more easily, explore ideas, and stay focused on performance and storytelling. Join Autodesk’s Animation and Research teams for an inside look at what’s next in animation at Autodesk and the tools we're building to give you more freedom to create the way you want to create. Through demos and research previews, you'll see new AI workflows for motion generation, alongside new approaches to story development and planning. You'll also see how Autodesk is building more flexible workflows that support artist choice, control, and interoperability. Presenters Lance Thornton Autodesk Noa Kaplan Autodesk Research Evan Atherton Autodesk Research Frederik Brudy Autodesk Research

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: Hands-On Machine Learning with Slang: Building High-Performance GPU Inference Pipelines
description: Learn to build GPU ML inference pipelines with Slang. Use a lightweight, open set of pre-written kernels to implement an MNIST CNN from scratch: load SafeTensors weights, run inference, and apply kernel fusion for better performance. Hands-on, cross-platform, and highly customizable—ideal for games, tools, and research.

title: LA Local Artists Showcase
description: The Spatial Storytelling LED Wall Showcase features a curated collection of short-form digital works by Los Angeles–based artists. Spanning immersive media, experimental film, generative art, and digital storytelling, the showcase highlights creative practices that explore new relationships between narrative, technology, and space. Join us between sessions and during the dedicated lunch hour to experience these works at architectural scale and celebrate the vibrant artistic community of Los Angeles within the Spatial Storytelling program. LA Local Artists: Narae Kim Artwork Title: Flowly Learn more: https://www.kimnarae.com Yara Feghali Artwork Title: Be.Longing XR Michaela Ternasky-Holland Artwork Title: Kapwa Learn more: @michaelaternaskyholland / www.michaelaternaskyholland.com @sisiigodly / https://aaron.work/ Faith “Aya” Umoh Artwork Title: Noösphere Learn more: www.FaithAyaUmoh.com Isabel Beavers Artwork Title: Aphrodite's Dream Learn more: www.isabelbeavers.com / @isabelbeavers Wadooah Wali Artwork Title: The Obscura Society Learn more: www.newcanvas.co / www.instagram.com/newcanvas_xr / www.linkedin.com/company/newcanvas Huntrezz Janos Artwork Title: AfroHungaria Learn more

title: Poster Sessions
description: New ideas start here. Join us to hear from authors as they discuss their work.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - MUSIC: Learning Muscle-Driven Dexterous Hand Control
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: MUSIC: Learning Muscle-Driven Dexterous Hand Control We present a data-driven framework for physics-based, muscle-driven dexterous control that enables musculoskeletal hands to perform piano playing for novel musical scores. Our framework adopts a hierarchical architecture, combining high-frequency muscle control with low-frequency coordination. The method achieves coordinated bimanual motions and produces human-like activation patterns, generalizing beyond reference data. Paper Presentation Presented: Thursday, July 23, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: Learning in Motion; Room 403 A

title: Animation Theater 2: Historically Speaking
description: The past is never just the past, it's an argument about the present. This program brings together works that mine history, memory, and inherited struggle to illuminate the fault lines we're still standing on.

title: Agentic Workflows
description: A participant-driven discussion on leveraging AI agents to enhance studio technology and workflow throughput. The goal is to reduce friction in current established studio pipelines by using AI for intervention, answering questions, and solving 'slow' issues, without requiring the removal or changing of existing pipelines. The discussion will explicitly avoid creative and pixel generation topics, focusing solely on augmenting Studio pipelines. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud, Pipeline, and Storage. Attendees will receive invites to our 'Beers of a Feather' event, the same evening.

title: Ancestral Craft and Emerging Technologies: Designing Futures from Place
description: This talk presents a place-based approach to designing interactive systems that combine ancestral craft and emerging technologies. Through XR installations and robotic artworks developed in the Amazon rainfores, it shares methods for building non-generic futures grounded in local knowledge, material practices, collaboration and real-time graphics.

title: Computer Graphics & Animation Research Projects
description: Now in its fifth year, the Computer Graphics & Animation Research Projects program develops research initiatives in computer graphics and animation, then invites undergraduate students, in all disciplines, worldwide, to join our research project teams. We also invite faculty to join as project advisors. So come, hear about our projects, and share your experiences in research at the undergraduate level.

title: Computer Graphics History: SIGGRAPH Stories and more…
description: Love SIGGRAPH’s history? Curious about the people, projects, and milestones that shaped modern computer graphics? Join the ACM SIGGRAPH History Committee Birds of a Feather for a fun, informal gathering where attendees swap stories and share memories about SIGGRAPH, past and present. Whether you have a favorite conference moment, a behind-the-scenes anecdote, or a burning question about the early days of the field, this is the place to hear it and tell it. Come meet fellow history enthusiasts, learn what the committee has been working on and pitch ideas for new projects to help preserve SIGGRAPH's history.

title: LTX | From Pixels to Physics: Building AI's Right Brain in the Open
description: Foundational models aren't new, but building LTX taught us their scope is expanding faster and further than anyone predicted. LTX is our multimodal generative world model, and in this talk Ofir shows how a system trained to do one deceptively simple thing, generate pixels across time, a.k.a. video, becomes a physically grounded foundation that reasons about light, physics, and causality. If language models are the left brain of AI, LTX is a bet on the right: the intuitive, perceptual, physical side of intelligence. We'll trace LTX's journey from a state-of-the-art open-weights model to core infrastructure across domains, from media and entertainment to physical AI. Expect real applications spanning robotics and simulation, a look under the hood at the architectural shifts that made it possible (MoE, stronger text encoders), and the conviction driving the project: when a model is open and production-ready, it compounds, for researchers, developers, enterprises, and the applications this room will build next.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Career Growth in Physical AI: Bringing 3D Graphics Skills into Robotics and Digital Twins
description: Creative 3D skills are becoming increasingly relevant to physical AI. Join this career growth panel to hear how industry leaders have moved from 3D graphics and content creation into roles across robotics, AI, simulation, and digital twins. Panelists will share what carried over, what they had to learn, and practical advice for others looking to make the same move. Presenters Ashley Goldstein NVIDIA Anthony Okuchi Keywords Studios Fatima Anes NVIDIA Laurie Koenig Miris

title: OMBI: Open Standards for the Metaverse Browser
description: AR glasses are arriving. Airports, hospitals, logistics centers, and universities are already planning spatial deployments, but the open infrastructure layer that makes it all work doesn't exist yet. The Open Metaverse Browser Initiative (OMBI), a Metaverse Standards Forum initiative seeded by RP1's operational prototype, is building that layer: an open-source Metaverse Browser Engine (Sneeze) that connects any device to spatial services on demand, on open standards from Khronos, W3C, and OGC, with no proprietary lock-in. This BOF brings together industry contributors to walk through the architecture, share progress, and ensure the AR hardware race produces a genuinely open spatial web.

title: Real-Time Shading BOF: Building a Community Around Shaders, Shading Languages, and Tech Artist Workflows
description: The Real-Time Shading BOF, with Ken Perlin and Marc Olano, aims to foster a vibrant, cross-disciplinary community of researchers, technical artists, hardware vendors, compiler developers, and practitioners working with shaders and shading languages. Building on the 2026 Shading Languages Symposium, this session will focus on practical pain points faced by technical artists in production environments—such as shader authoring, debugging, portability, performance, and integration with modern real-time pipelines—while welcoming insights from language designers and hardware experts. The BOF will serve as an informal forum for discussion, idea exchange, and networking, acting as a forerunner to the Real-Time Shading Symposium in 2027.

title: SIGGRAPH for Health: Convergence of Trends and Techniques
description: SIGGRAPH is the perfect platfom to create impact in a world that increasingly needs care! This session is the most desired follow-up to the conversations on how the convergence of AI, computer graphics, and immersive media is transforming healthcare. To ensure adoption, these innovations must combine science and human-centered perspectives. Gathering research, technology and culture, SIGGRAPH is a core player in this domain. Join the opening session to hear inputs from leading experts sharing strategies for obtaining international impact, behind the scenes of innovation ecosystems, real case studies, and human centered approach. We will have a networking cocktail, followed by the interactive workshop—help shape SIGGRAPH’s strategy for health innovation!

title: Autodesk Vision Series | AI in the Production Pipeline: A Conversation with Cinesite's CTO
description: Join Cinesite CTO Michele Sciolette for a fireside chat with Kamal Mistry, Sr. Director of Product for Autodesk Media & Entertainment, on how one of the world’s leading animation and VFX studios are approaching AI in production. The conversation will explore where AI can remove friction from creative workflows, how teams evaluate emerging tools for real pipeline use, and what it takes to introduce new capabilities without disrupting artists’ craft, collaboration, or creative control. Presenters Michele Sciolette Cinesite Kamal Mistry Autodesk

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - Sketch2Arti: Sketch-based Articulation Modeling of CAD Objects
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: Sketch2Arti: Sketch-based Articulation Modeling of CAD Objects Sketch2Arti is the first sketch-based articulation modeling system for CAD objects. Given a CAD model and simple 2D sketches, it identifies movable parts and predicts motion parameters. It can also complete missing internal structures revealed by motion, enabling controllable, iterative, and generalizable articulation modeling for diverse objects. Paper Presentation Presented: Wednesday, July 22, 10:45 am-12:35 pm; Paper Session: Vectors Graphics, Strokes & Sketches; Room 403 A

title: Advances in Real-time Rendering in Games, Part II
description: Modern video games employ a variety of sophisticated algorithms to produce groundbreaking 3D rendering pushing the visual boundaries and interactive experience of rich environments. This course brings state-of-the-art and production-proven rendering techniques for fast, interactive rendering of complex and engaging virtual worlds of video games. This is the course to attend if you are in the game development industry or want to learn the latest and greatest techniques in the real-time rendering domain!

title: AI and Agency
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Chaos | From Game Engine to Production Engine
description: For many virtual production facilities, game engines became the foundation upon which modern LED volume workflows were built. Along the way, teams developed new disciplines, new processes, and new technical expertise to bridge the gap between real-time rendering and production requirements. This session shares StradaXR’s perspective on that journey and examines emerging approaches that seek to reduce workflow complexity, preserve creative intent, and bring production-ready content closer to the stage.

title: Computational Photography
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Efficient 3D Content Creation for Immersive Experiences
description: This workshop is dedicated to democratizing 3D content creation, encompassing both static and dynamic 3D assets, by exploring cutting-edge advances in volumetric video built on 4D Gaussian Splatting, fast 3D objects and scenes reconstruction, AI generated 3D content, and real-time immersive video generation with diffusion models. We bring together both academic and industrial researchers and practitioners to discuss scalable pipelines that lower the barrier to immersive content creation. What's more, we will explore the 3D/4D content experience on both VR headsets as well as consumer mobile platforms. A key feature of this workshop is a hands-on demonstration: participants will experience 1) volumetric video of industrial demonstration in both video-on-demand applications and in real-time streaming of live-broadcasting, 2) interactive experience of AI generated 3D content and 3D reconstruction, and also 3) real-time interactive and high-resolution video generation via diffusion models. The workshop seeks to bridge the gap between cutting-edge 3D content creation in academic research and in real-world industrial applications.

title: Global-Scale Photorealism with Google Immersive View XR
description: This talk chronicles the engineering and design journey of bringing Google Maps Immersive View to standalone XR. We detail the unique challenges of rendering this fully navigable, global-scale environment on constrained headsets. Highlighting rendering optimizations for 3D Tiles and 3D Gaussian Splatting, intuitive hands-only gestural navigation, and adaptive spatial audio, we reveal how to achieve performant photorealism and active user presence on next-generation XR hardware.

title: Light Transport

title: Motion Generation, Warping & Control
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Building Physics-Aware, RTX-Enabled Apps with NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries and AI Agents
description: Join Damien Fagnou for a hands-on look at building physics-aware, RTX-enabled simulation apps with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. Starting from an existing 3D application, Damien will show how developers can connect OpenUSD scenes, RTX rendering, physics, simulation, and UI capabilities into a working prototype. Lexi, an AI agent co-host, will help show how agents can inspect scenes, call tools, and speed up iteration while Omniverse libraries provide the foundation for repeatable physical AI workflows in 3D graphics and simulation.

title: Parametric Surfaces
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Performance Focus
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: RigX: Rethinking How Characters Are Rigged
description: Marko Š. presents RigX, a next-generation character rigging and deformation pipeline designed to rethink how digital humans are built for high-end games and cinematic production. Unlike traditional approaches that rely on heavy muscle simulations or complex dependency chains, RigX introduces a lightweight logic-driven deformation system that produces muscle-like behavior with full artistic control and significantly reduced computational overhead. In this session, attendees will see how RigX enables the creation of both full-body and facial systems with consistent, production-ready performance across a wide range of character styles, from hyper-realistic digital doubles to stylized or cartoony characters. The system is designed for responsiveness, stability, and scalability in real-time pipelines. The presentation includes a short rendered breakdown followed by a live demonstration of facial animation, body deformation, and rig behavior in real time. Marko will walk through core design principles, integration strategies for advanced production pipelines, and practical considerations for adopting RigX in studio environments. The session concludes with a focus on production impact, de

title: SIGGRAPH Swag Swap and Archive Donation Location
description: Do you have old SIGGRAPH collectibles or other swag you no longer want? Are you missing that one SIGGRAPH pin and you can’t live without it? Come to the SIGGRAPH Swag Swap and you can trade items with other attendees. You may also donate unwanted items such as promotional materials, collectibles, publications, and electronic media to the SIGGRAPH History Archive at the Swag Swap. Please check the archive first at https://history.siggraph.org/inventory-view-all/ to see if your item is needed.

title: Studio Storage: This is an annual discussion of VFX & Animation studios' Large-Scale Storage experiences
description: Studio Storage: Storage for VFX & Animation has extreme requirements and only a limited number of proven solutions are available or you can build your own. Industry experts and new entrants are all welcomed, for a sharing of knowledge, experience, and best practices. Mail joe@jdamato.com for any questions or comments. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the VFX Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud Native, Pipeline, Studio Storage, and Agentic Workflows.

title: USD in production
description: This hands-on course focuses on debugging USD composition in production. Participants gain practical experience authoring data across composition arcs and diagnosing issues in complex scenes. Building on USD in Production, the course emphasizes interactive use of modern debugging and visualization tools to understand how opinions, overrides, and data resolve.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Clumsy, Deadly, Unforgettable: Creating the Rad Roach Massacre in Fallout Season 2
description: Join Refuge VFX's founder Fred Ruff and Lead Look Development Artist Stephen Lauser as they break down the creation of the rad roach massacre sequence from Fallout Season 2. From early creative challenges and budget-driven decisions to creature animation, environmental interaction, and final compositing, we'll reveal how a swarm of giant insects became one of the sequence's most memorable and terrifying moments. Through behind-the-scenes footage and shot breakdowns, you'll see how the team transformed clumsy creatures into deadly predators while balancing artistic goals with production realities. Presenters Fred Ruff Refuge VFX Stephen Lauser Refuge VFX

title: Deploying Radiance Fields Across Real-time and Web: Bridging Academia and Industry
description: The session is for attendees interested in information exchange between the research and industry/application communities working with different implementations and applications of radiance fields. The third community-driven forum features a panel composed of leading researchers presenting at 2026 and industry representatives, plus an audience Q&A session. It aims to foster knowledge exchange, identify future directions, and promote new collaborations, connecting the research and user communities within computer graphics and interactive techniques in applying NeRF/3DGS for visualization, digital twins, interactive media (XR/3D/web) design. The discussion will dive into data compression, web-based hosting, game engine integration, & dynamic techniques (4DGS).

title: Sponsored Keynote by Bolt Graphics: Darwesh Singh, Founder, CEO/CTO, Building a GPU for the Future of Computer Graphics
description: What happens when you design a GPU to be very efficient at path tracing and physics simulations? It ends up looking different from any other GPU. GPUs were originally designed to be proficient at rasterization. However, the holy grail of computer graphics is real-time path tracing and simulations, which so far has been difficult to achieve with GPUs built around rasterization. Learn how Bolt Graphics is building its very own GPU, Zeus, to achieve real-time path tracing and simulations for creators and researchers around the world. Contributor Darwesh Singh Bolt Graphics

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - A LoD of Gaussians: Out-of-Core Training and Rendering for Seamless Ultra-Large Scene Reconstruction
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: A LoD of Gaussians: Out-of-Core Training and Rendering for Seamless Ultra-Large Scene Reconstruction Gaussian Splatting struggles with large scenes due to memory limits and chunking artifacts. We introduce "A LoD of Gaussians," a new framework enabling ultra-large scene training and rendering on a single consumer GPU. Through out-of-core streaming and Level-of-Detail, it achieves seamless multi-scale reconstruction without relying on scene partitioning. Paper Presentation Presented: Monday, July 20, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: 3D Gaussian Splatting I; Room 408 A

title: Hands-on Course: Hands-on Programming and Running Quantum Teleportation
description: In this hands-on programming course you'll learn how to write and run quantum computing code. Then you'll program the quantum teleportation algorithm, and run it on a high-quality simulator to transfer the state of a quantum bit to your partner, who will decode it to get your secret message.

title: Chaos | Cloud Arena: Bringing Real-Time Ray-Traced Virtual Production to the Global Cloud
description: As Virtual Production continues to transform filmmaking, the demand for scalable, accessible infrastructure is growing beyond the walls of traditional studios. In this session, Eric Pinkel of CenterGrid will present Cloud Arena, a collaborative research initiative exploring how real-time ray-traced Virtual Production workflows can be deployed across globally distributed cloud infrastructure. Using CenterGrid VFX Datacenter and Chaos Arena, the team successfully validated cloud-hosted rendering, cloud-hosted Arena services, and international operator workflows spanning North America and Europe. The presentation will walk through the project's milestone-based development process, system architecture, technical challenges, and key findings, including why synchronization and deterministic frame delivery have emerged as the next major frontier for cloud-native Virtual Production. Attendees will gain insight into how cloud-based GPU workstations, remote rendering, and globally connected creative teams can expand access to Virtual Production technologies while enabling new workflows for studios, educators, and content creators worldwide.

title: Global CG Pipelines
description: A casual discussion on production pipeline development. Come talk shop about the latest trends, common concerns, and best practices. This is part of a linked series of Technical Pipeline BoFs, covering the Reference Platform, Renderfarming, Cloud, Pipeline, and Storage. Attendees will receive invites to our 'Beers of a Feather' event, the same evening.

title: Integrating Neural Graphics into Vulkan Rendering Pipelines on Arm: A Fireside Conversation with the Teams Behind Neural Dawn - Arm x Sumo
description: "Neural graphics techniques have moved into production rendering workflows in games and this is becoming true in mobile. This fireside-style session brings together engineers and developers from Arm and Sumo Digital to discuss the development of Neural Dawn, a production-quality Unreal Engine 5 mobile title built using Arm Neural Technology. The discussion focuses on the practical realities of integrating neural graphics into Vulkan rendering pipelines for mobile hardware. Topics include workload scheduling, frame graph integration, memory bandwidth constraints, debugging, performance validation, scalability across device classes, and balancing visual quality against demanding performance targets. Panelists will also examine how Arm Vulkan extensions and neural technologies influenced rendering and engine decisions during development, alongside broader ecosystem considerations such as portability, tooling, and preparing for emerging neural graphics hardware capabilities. Drawing on perspectives from engine developers, artists, and production teams, the session provides a grounded view of what it takes to move neural graphics from prototype to shipping content. Attendees will leave

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day: Autonomous Vehicle Simulation Technologies with NuRec, 3D Gaussian, Cosmos-Dreams
description: Last year generating five seconds of photorealistic simulated driving took minutes of compute. Today, you can interactively drive through a world that has never existed in real time, rendered frame by frame on a single GPU. This talk covers two complementary approaches that make this possible: neural scene reconstruction from real-world captures with NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec and generative scenario synthesis with NVIDIA Cosmos-Dreams. It will cover open problems and solutions in scene representation, temporal consistency, and controllable generation, all core computer graphics challenges. Presenters Jialiang Wang NVIDIA Itai Zadok NVIDIA Darian Hogue Mcity

title: Running large-scale, multi-site VFX productions in the real world: what's working & what to fix?
description: This BOF session is a candid conversation about navigating today’s global VFX landscape. We’ll move beyond tax incentive headlines to focus on what truly makes geographically-distributed productions succeed: robust creative pipelines, effective cross-site collaboration, and empowering technology. We’ll discuss practical realities coordinating work across timezones and cultures, examining how to harness the diverse strengths of global teams to turn geographical distribution into an advantage. We'll address client expectations, how studios balance tax incentive pressures with maintaining creative quality, and how technology helps companies prepare for a future without boundaries. Together, let’s build the roadmap for our industry’s next chapter.

title: The Future of Web3D: Standards, Performance, and Immersive Experiences
description: As browser capabilities expand with WebGPU, spatial computing becomes mainstream, and AI transforms content creation, we're at a pivotal moment for open 3D standards. Whether you're building immersive web experiences, developing 3D tools, or shaping the future of open standards, this is your opportunity to connect with peers, share insights, and influence the direction of Web3D technologies. Come prepared to discuss what's working, what needs improvement, and where we go next.

title: Roll for Reality: Virtual Production Improvised D&D Show
description: Roll for Reality is a live, improvised Dungeons & Dragons show integrating tabletop roleplaying, motion capture, AI-assisted narrative tools, and real-time XR visualization. The project explores hybrid XR storytelling frameworks that connect improvisational performance with live virtual production environments.

title: Start, Join, Connect: All About ACM SIGGRAPH Chapters
description: This casual information session will highlight the impact of ACM SIGGRAPH Chapters around the world, provide guidance on how to start or join a professional or student chapter, and offer opportunities to connect and engage with chapter leaders and members of the Professional & Student Chapters Committee (PSCC). More information about the PSCC, chapters near you and how to get involved can be found at https://siggraph.org/chapters.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Award Presentations and Talks
description: Each year, ACM SIGGRAPH presents nine awards recognizing exceptional achievements in computer graphics and interactive techniques at the ACM SIGGRAPH Conference. For a list of the awardees, visit: https://www.siggraph.org/awards/

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Crafting Characters Beyond the Reef: Translating Moana’s Animated World into Live Action with ILM
description: Join Industrial Light & Magic Animation Supervisor Maia Kayser for a behind-the-scenes look at the character animation of Disney’s live-action Moana. This presentation explores the technical and artistic challenges of translating the beloved animated world into a photorealistic cinematic experience – crafting digital characters and performances that are both physically grounded and emotionally expressive.

title: Orchestrating Multimodal Control for Unified Audio-Visual Synthesis

Beyond text prompts, we demonstrate precise control via audio, video, imagery, and spatial data. Attendees will learn dual-stream transformer architectural innovations and practical techniques for orchestrating high-fidelity, aligned multimodal generation

title: The Previs Battleground: Precision Planning vs Creative Freedom
description: This panel examines how previs is evolving across Animation, VFX, and Games, and the trade-offs between fast, exploratory workflows and high-fidelity planning. Industry leaders share how different previs strategies shape creative iteration, technical pipelines, budgets, and downstream production decisions in real-world environments. Moderator Megha Davalath Netflix Animation Studios Panelists Todd Jansen Netflix Animation Studios Adam Holmes Sony Pictures Imageworks Karl Herbst Sony Pictures Animation Jon Teer Blizzard Entertainment Dave Clayton Weta FX Kyle Winkelman Industrial Light & Magic

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - AGILE: Hand-object Interaction Reconstruction from Video via Agentic Generation
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: AGILE: Hand-object Interaction Reconstruction from Video via Agentic Generation AGILE reconstructs 3D hand-object interactions from monocular videos via agentic generation. It produces complete, simulation-ready object meshes and robustly estimates object pose without SfM, enabling accurate and stable results on challenging real-world data. Paper Presentation Presented: Monday, July 20, 3:45-5:35 pm; Paper Session: Humans & Hands - Reconstruction; Room 403 A

title: Building the Interactive Future: Inside Tripo's Full-Stack 3D Generation Ecosystem
description: While text and image AI have seamlessly integrated into daily workflows, 3D generation remains uniquely challenging. In this session, the Tripo AI research team examines why 3D demands specialized architectures, and demonstrates the full-stack generative ecosystem built to address it. Moving beyond the generation of unstructured meshes and baked textures, the team will detail Tripo's latest foundational models, highlighting H-series for high-fidelity geometry and texturing, alongside P-series for extremely fast, production-grade mesh topology. Through live demonstrations, attendees will see how these architectures output clean assets complete with semantic segmentation and automatic rigging, i.e., capabilities that are directly integrated into pipelines for gaming, 3D printing, and interactive media. The session concludes by shifting focus from object generation to environment simulation. Leveraging massive-scale architectures and multimodal integration, the Tripo team will preview their ongoing R&D in Generative World Models, demonstrating how the field is advancing toward dynamic environments, agentic systems, and world modeling

title: Montage as Pedagogical Scaﬀold for Reasoning Beyond Language
description: Multimodal reasoning models have introduced a disruptive, still-insufficiently elaborated shift in how students learn and create–functioning as what some might call a "collaborative partner." The crux of the issue, however, is that this human-machine interaction carries an inherent asymmetry: the machine holds a partial view of context, stakes, and tacit/embodied knowledge involved. This BoF gathers practitioners to share strategies for working and teaching in algorithmically opaque creative environments. As an opening provocation, we'll explore possibilities latent-space visualization tools bring for latent-space navigation, then open the floor: What tacit knowledge manifests here? What pedagogies are emerging in your practice?

title: NVIDIA Physical AI Day | Introduction to Newton: Open-Source GPU Physics for Robotics
description: Researchers and developers need faster, physically grounded simulation to model complex interactions and close the sim-to-real gap across diverse robot embodiments. Newton is an open-source, GPU-accelerated physics engine built on NVIDIA Warp. With OpenUSD integration, Newton enables real-time simulation of rigid and soft bodies, contact, friction, and actuators for advancing robot learning and simulation research

title: AI3D Frontiers: AI for 3D BOF #4
description: Your favorite non-commercial, honestly live-demo-focused BOF continues as a place for polymath hackers, artists, researchers, and independent creators to show what they are actually building — from XR to SOTA gen AI 3D agents to the strange, early, hybrid things that do not yet fit neatly into a paper, product category, or formal session. Come share what you are working on, compare notes from the frontier, and help define what AI for 3D is becoming.

title: Sustainable Research in Computer Graphics
description: From climate change to biodiversity loss and resource exhaustion, human activities are impacting Earth’s limits and Computer Graphics is no exception. How can our research practices and organizations evolve to respect these limits? After the success of last year’s BoF, we continue building a community of people who want to think about the broader impacts of our research and how to collectively work towards a more sustainable future. This interactive meetup session will allow attendees to share experiences and questions on related topics in small groups, regardless of their current levels of involvement or expertise in sustainable approaches to research.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Pioneers Reception
description: Yearly Pioneers social gathering with light appetizers and a cash bar. No agenda, just schmoozing with old friends and colleagues. Admission by Pioneer membership as printed on your badge.

title: Real-Time Live!
description: Real-Time Live! Meet and Greet Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers immediately following the session. This is a great opportunity to continue the conversation, connect with fellow attendees, and ask any additional questions. Location: Hall K Lobby

title: Frontier Talk: Towards a Graphics-Silicon Gymnasium to Shape the Future of Micro-Embodied AI
description: The graphics community thrives on standardized APIs and accessible GPU silicon. However, a parallel frontier awaits in micro-embodied AI, where tiny units capture images, perform mental geometric simulations, and intelligently act under constraints. However, the traditional graphics research abstraction breaks down in this regime. GPUs do not fit, and diverse custom silicon lacks common APIs. Confined to simulation, graphics researchers cannot access physical experimentation to explore their native challenges. This talk envisions a graphics-silicon gymnasium to bridge the gap and unlock fundamental questions: What new graphics algorithms emerge under the unique constraints of tiny, specialized silicon? What minimum properties are required for mental geometric simulations at miniature scales? We walk through semiconductor history to confirm absolute physical constraints, explore an illustrative camera-equipped smart ingestible pill, and discuss the merits of a gymnasium environment that can empower the graphics community to shape the future of micro-embodied AI.

title: Pathfinders
description: FIRST-TIME ATTENDEE RESOURCE: Pathfinders will be available to help you find your way. Pathfinders is a volunteer group dedicated to mentoring first-time conference visitors. The Pathfinders team can offer detailed explanations and descriptions of all SIGGRAPH programs or venues, and help you build a schedule based on your personal skills and interests.

title: AI for Creative Visual Content Generation, Editing and Understanding
description: The 10th installment of the CVEU workshop series marks a transformative shift toward the Next-Generation Creator-Machine Co-Learning Paradigm. Organized by leading academic researchers and the technical architects behind current video generation models, this workshop explores a future where generative AI evolves from a passive tool into a collaborative partner that internalizes both physical laws and artistic intent. Our primary objective is to bridge the "semantic gap" between raw visual tokens and the sophisticated language of cinema, focusing on how World Models can balance physical factuality with creative license. We will investigate how machine learning translate abstract user intent into precise model behavior, effectively reconstructing traditional production pipelines for professional filmmakers and artists alike. Attendees can expect a high-energy program featuring technical demos of AI-native creative workflows, provocative keynote sessions, and an open "Research-meets-Art" roundtable designed to spark dialogue between model builders and visual storytellers. This forum offers a unique opportunity to engage directly with the creators of the world’s most advanced visual AI

title: Building the Future of Web Graphics with WebGL and WebGPU
description: Join the Khronos Group, browser developers, and the web graphics community for an interactive look at the latest advances in WebGL and WebGPU. Explore new capabilities for real time rendering, compute, AI, tooling, and cross platform deployment, along with the evolving ecosystem across browsers, frameworks, game engines, and immersive web experiences. Hear from developers building the future of 3D on the web, ask questions, share feedback, and help shape what comes next for web graphics.

title: Current Hiring Trends in the Animation & VFX Industry
description: This panel brings together recruiting leaders from major animation and VFX studios to share first-person insight into contemporary hiring realities. Through candid discussion, panelists clarify evolving skill demands, internship and entry-level pathways, and recruitment processes, offering artists, students, and educators a clearer, more realistic snapshot of an industry in transition. Moderator C. K. Olsen University of Iowa Panelists Kathleen Ruffalo Framestore Tiffany Purkeypile LAIKA Caitlin Dietz None Benjamin Noble The Third Floor

It introduces key Vulkan concepts and provides comprehensive guidance for writing and modernizing Vulkan applications in 2026, focusing on resource, pipeline, draw, and synchronization management.

title: New 3D creation workflows and international collaboration in the real-time 3D and GenAI Era
description: This BOF explores how 3D production workflows are evolving across the world and how these changes are reshaping collaboration between studios, toolmakers and creative teams. Topics include interoperability, production organization, real-time 3D and the growing role of generative AI in creative pipelines. Rather than opposing regional approaches, the session will examine shared challenges, emerging practices and opportunities for collaboration. Relevant to the SIGGRAPH community, it addresses workflow design, production technology and AI integration. The session targets studio leaders, production managers, technical directors, CG supervisors, developers, researchers, educators and creators working with modern 3D pipelines.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Ray Trace Your Way To A Better Life with Omniverse Libraries, OpenUSD, Agents, and You
description: Welcome to the first course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp. Physical AI systems need visual worlds that can be rendered, inspected, queried, and trusted before they are used for simulation, robotics testing, or training. This hands-on course introduces that foundation by guiding attendees through building an OpenUSD-based RTX viewport with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and AI-agent-assisted development. Starting from a minimal Python renderer, attendees will create an interactive app that loads USD scenes, displays RTX output, exposes camera views, queries scene attributes, and writes image results. They will also learn a repeatable agent workflow: define the goal, provide context, name the relevant skills, review the generated diff, and run only after the code is understood. By the end, participants will have a working RTX viewport application and a practical method for using AI agents to build reliable graphics and simulation tools for physical AI workflows.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Applied Research for Ray Tracing Massive Scenes in Real Time
description: Rendering massive, dynamic environments in real time requires new approaches to processing and managing geometry on the GPU. This session presents applied research from the NVIDIA Developer Technology team, including real-time massive forest simulation, RTX Mega Geometry for large-scale scene complexity, and Gaussian splatting for novel view synthesis. It also covers new approaches for GPU-accelerated curve and tube rendering, along with engineering best practices for scaling real-time 3D scene rendering with glTF. Presenters Tristan Lorach NVIDIA Brian Barran NVIDIA

title: Poisson Problems in Computer Graphics
description: Like a semester long graduate seminar on Poisson Problems in Computer Graphics, this course looks at Poisson problems through the lens of 13 technical papers selected by the lecturers. The lecturers will highlight trends, similarities, differences, and historical threads through the papers.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Animating Weapon Skins for VALORANT with Riot Games
description: Players spend hours unlocking the next weapon skin. Now, see firsthand the hours that go into creating those prized skins for VALORANT, Riot Games’ award-winning first-person tactical hero shooter game. Senior Animator, Brendan Russert, shares what it takes to create gun skin animations that feel sharp, unique, and worthy of a clutch moment. He'll cover the artistic decisions behind expressive weapon motion, the gameplay and technical limitations behind these animations, practical Maya tips and tricks, and how those assets make their way into the engine.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | From Viewport to Virtual Production: Real-Time Ray Tracing with Chaos Vantage
description: Production USD assets can now be ray traced interactively as they move from look development into virtual production. This session explores how artists and technical teams can use Chaos Vantage, including NVIDIA DLSS, through its Hydra delegate in SideFX Solaris to render natively assembled OpenUSD scenes and Gaussian splat assets directly in the viewport. Muhammed Hamed will discuss how this pipeline helps bridge the gap between look development and on-set production, with a look at scaling assets into on-set decision-making using Chaos Arena.

title: The Fate of Humanity rests with Project Hail Mary. ILM's Contribution to visual effects behind the film
description: Swarms of spacefaring alien microbes, an infected star, two one-of-a-kind spacecraft, a reluctant astronaut, and the construction of a never-before-seen star system. These are just some of the challenges that the ILM visual effects team, overseen by Production VFX Supervisors Paul Lambert and Mag Sarnowska, was tasked with creating for Christopher Miller and Phil Lord’s blockbuster film, Project Hail Mary. Join the Industrial Light & Magic team as they launch into the technical, creative, and scientific challenges faced by the crew in asset construction, compositing, development, and visualizing the real-time effects and virtual production work required to bring the story to life. Introduction by: Paul Lambert and Mag Sarnowskaare, Production Visual Effects Supervisors. Presenters Tristan Myles Visual Effects Supervisor Industrial Light & Magic Shaun Roth Associate VFX Supervisor Industrial Light & Magic Ron Radeztsky Astrometric Systems Modeling Engineer Lucasfilm

title: Art Gallery
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - GauSmoke: Hybrid Physics-Optical Gaussian Splatting for Sparse Smoke Reconstruction
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: GauSmoke: Hybrid Physics-Optical Gaussian Splatting for Sparse Smoke Reconstruction We present a physics-aware method for reconstructing dynamic fluids from sparse-view videos. By integrating volumetric rendering with physically guided Gaussian optimization, it enforces consistency in density and motion, reducing artifacts and improving realism. The approach achieves accurate, stable 3D smoke reconstruction with high visual and physical fidelity. Paper Presentation Presented: Tuesday, July 21, 9-10:30 am; Paper Session: Fluid Reconstruction & Optimization; Room 408 A

title: Gaussian Splatting for Animation: Emerging Workflows and Hybrid Pipelines
description: Gaussian Splatting is emerging as a high-fidelity, real-time alternative to traditional 3D representations, with growing relevance for captured environments, performances, and animation workflows. This Birds of a Feather session invites technical directors, animators, researchers, and tool developers to discuss how Gaussian-based representations can integrate into production pipelines, from acquisition and layout to lighting, editing, and delivery. Topics include hybrid mesh/splat workflows, animated representations, interoperability, volumetric capture, and stop-motion digitization. The session will emphasize shared challenges, emerging practices, and open exchange across studios, academia, and tool developers.

title: glTF 2.1 and Beyond: Building Interactive and Spatial 3D Experiences
description: glTF is evolving beyond static asset delivery into an open foundation for interactiveand spatial 3D experiences. Join the Khronos Group and the glTF community for an interactive look at the roadmap toward glTF 2.1 and beyond, including new features for interactivity and physics, advances in tooling and sample implementations, use cases and updates to glTF Gaussian splatting capabilities, PBR evolution, volumetric media capture and delivery, and new opportunities for real time and geospatial applications. Bring your questions, share feedback, and help shape the future of open 3D.

title: Hands-on Course: Accelerate Robot Learning With NVIDIA Isaac Lab and Newton
description: Hands-on course on Newton, a GPU-accelerated physics engine, and its integration with Isaac Lab for robot learning. Covers Newton’s architecture and import pipeline, solver and contact tuning, articulation and sensors, then training and evaluating policies in Isaac Lab with Newton as the physics backend.

This session covers real-time rendering with RTX and DLSS, along with GPU-accelerated Redshift production workflows in Maxon Cinema 4D and other DCC applications, including Houdini, Maya, and 3ds Max. Attendees will learn how to shorten iteration cycles, enhance visual fidelity, and deliver compelling imagery that wins clients and elevates creative output. Presenters Rick Barrett Maxon James Mauger Maxon

title: Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) Best Practices: On-Prem and Cloud for VFX and Animation
description: As VFX and Animation studios have embraced remote work, Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) is key. Join our Birds of a Feather session to explore on-prem and cloud-based VDI instances. We'll delve into the evolving opportunities and considerations for technology teams that arise with this infrastructure shift, sharing knowledge, experiences, and best practices for this evolving landscape. This is an open forum with Industry experts and newcomers alike are invited to contribute to this vital discussion. For questions, email joe@jdamato.com

title: Ai War Cloud Database
description: AI War Cloud Database catalogues the systems used to make automated decisions in warfare, and it maps where the same types of tools appear in smartphones and popular social platforms. This demonstration presents critical and technical decisions that were hand-in-hand, central to research, information design, and proliferation of the experience.

Spanning interactive installation, AI, robotics, bio-art, data portraiture, ecological simulation, digital printmaking, and experimental animation, these talks offer a behind-the-scenes view into the creative processes and critical questions shaping contemporary technologically mediated art. title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Rendering’s Next Frontier with OTOY
description: Emerging trends in GPU rendering are expanding what’s possible across motion graphics, filmmaking, product design, VFX, games, virtual production, immersive media, and beyond. This session looks at how advances in frontier AI technologies, workflows, and IP provenance tools are influencing the future of rendering. Attendees will gain a forward-looking view of how these trends are shaping creative industries.

title: Prana Labs | Seeing is Believing, Grasping is Physics: Generative Environments with Physically-Grounded Robot Manipulation
description: This demo is a collaboration between Tripo and Prana that pairs generative 3D content with physically grounded simulation for robot manipulation. Tripo generates the functional 3D assets and drives the generative rendering, producing convincing scenes equipped with simulation-ready geometry. Prana's simulation engine, FLASH, governs the physics of these environments, so contact, grasping, and object dynamics behave in a physically consistent way rather than only looking correct. Scenarios center on robotic manipulation, such as a robot arm picking up and handling everyday objects. Every interaction is resolved by the simulator, so the same scene that looks real also responds correctly when a gripper makes contact, including objects made of composite materials that combine rigid and flexible parts within a single body. The simulator is fully differentiable, so its physical behavior can be fit to real observations rather than tuned by hand. Functional 3D generation and physical accuracy are distinct but complementary problems. Tripo's generation provides the precise 3D structure and visual diversity to generalize; Prana's physically grounded simulation makes it behave right with the

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Not Just Big, Alive: The Animation of Kong in Monarch Season 2
description: In Monarch: Legacy of Monsters Season 2, Rodeo FX’s animation team faced a massive challenge: make a 300-foot titan feel like a character. Kong had to emote and move with the weight of something real, not just big. Animation Supervisor Christophe St-Pierre Paradis walks through the creative and technical process behind Kong's performance in Autodesk Maya, from the motion capture sessions that formed the foundation of his movement, to the animator-driven refinement that transformed raw mocap data into a creature performance with genuine scale and emotional presence. The talk covers locomotion at an impossible scale, fight choreography rooted in intention, the micro-movements that give a giant an inner life, and the pipeline realities of preserving a performance through fur, cloth, and simulation.
