title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: SimReady or Not, Here Comes Gravity with OpenUSD for Simulation
description: Physics is at the heart of building reliable physical AI systems, and this course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp explores how to move from visual inspection into simulation readiness. Workflows in physical AI need assets that are structured, validated, and physically meaningful. Building on the prior OpenUSD and RTX viewport lab, this hands-on course moves from visual inspection into simulation readiness. Attendees will validate OpenUSD assets for simulation-ready (SimReady) workflows, convert CAD and Gaussian splat content to USD, create a physics stage, and assemble environments such as stairs, ramps, or other test layouts. They will enable physics, make scene objects interactable, and run simulations to see how assets behave under gravity and user input. By the end, attendees will have a runnable physics scene with validated assets, converted USD content, and an interactive environment ready for robotics testing or physical AI workflows.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Faster Creative Iteration with Autodesk Arnold
description: Creative iteration remains one of the most expensive challenges in modern content production. In this session, Autodesk will present recent advances in the Academy Award-winning Arnold renderer aimed at reducing technical friction in the creative process. The session will cover architectural improvements to interactive viewport rendering and GPU optimizations that improve responsiveness in complex scenes. Attendees will learn how these advances help artists and technical directors iterate more fluidly while maintaining the robustness, predictability, and image quality expected from a physically based renderer. Autodesk will also share lessons learned from real-world production deployments

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - MotionBricks: Scalable Real-Time Motions with Modular Latent Generative Model and Smart Primitives
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: MotionBricks: Scalable Real-Time Motions with Modular Latent Generative Model and Smart Primitives MotionBricks is a real-time generative framework that transforms interactive motion control for animation and robotics. By combining a large-scale latent backbone with intuitive "smart primitives," it delivers high-quality, zero-shot motion synthesis at 15,000 FPS, allowing users to effortlessly build complex and fine-grained animations and robotic applications. Paper Presentation Presented: Tuesday, July 21, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: Motion Generation, Warping & Control; Room 403 A

title: Toys meet Tech: The Making of Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5
description: Howdy folks! Hop onto your saddle and hold on tight because this is going to be a wild ride! Jessie is the new sheriff in town at Bonnie’s house, who is now eight years old. Ever since Bonnie’s parents gifted her with LilyPad, a grating, tablet tech toy shaped like a frog, playtime has dried up; Bonnie’s a full-on screen zombie! THE AGE OF TOYS IS OVER!! In this Production Session, you’ll learn how Toy Story 5 was made including new characters, new environments, new looks and new technology. We’ll show you how we created FIFTY Buzz Lightyears, new tech toys, a perky Sticky Hand, a glamorous collection of horse figurines, a haunting toy shed, and a child’s imagination brought to life on the big screen in a way the audience has never seen before. Join us on this adventure and learn how the world of Toy Story is taken to the next level of visual richness and technical innovation. Yeehaw!!! Presenters Thomas Jordan Visual Effects Supervisor Pixar Animation Studios Athena Xenakis Shading Supervisor Pixar Animation Studios Mariana Galindo Tailoring and Simulation Supervisor Pixar Animation Studios Stephen Marshall Effects Supervisor Pixar Animation Studios Paul Kanyuk Crowds Technical Su

title: VR Biosecurity Surveillance Training for Indigenous Rangers
description: In northern Australia, biosecurity is everybody's business. Learn how Mod designs a hybrid live action video / game production using graph data science to create an hour-long VR training experience for Indigenous rangers and stakeholders, identifying pests, viruses and diseases out in the field while remaining "croc-wise" in croc country! Contributor Michela Ledwidge Mod

title: ANARI: Advancing Open Rendering Standards
description: Get the latest updates directly from the Khronos ANARI Working Group as the ecosystem continues to expand across visualization, simulation, engineering, and high-performance computing. This session will cover recent technical progress, roadmap discussions, interoperability efforts, implementation updates, and opportunities for community participation. Learn how ANARI is evolving to support modern rendering workflows, scalable deployment across diverse hardware and software environments, and closer integration with adjacent Khronos technologies. Join the discussion, connect with contributors, and hear what is next for open, portable rendering standards.

title: Miris | OpenUSD streamed to any device, anywhere.
description: Fully path-traced, OpenPBR-compliant OpenUSD, streamed to any consumer device, anywhere. Reaching low-powered hardware has always meant reducing fidelity, downgrading geometry and texture sizes. That is exactly where photorealism dies. Miris doesn't work that way. We use AI to condition each 3D asset once upstream, optimizing across devices, network conditions, and content characteristics, then stream it adaptively to any device. Our radiance field technology, built leveraging NVIDIA fVDB software and CoreWeave infrastructure enables Miris to deliver high-fidelity content at global scale. Join Sean Looper, CTO of Miris, for a look at how the pipeline is built and how Playbook, Evercast, CGAxis, and Voxel51 are putting it to work across digital twins, physical AI, media and entertainment, and digital asset marketplaces. If you build with 3D, come talk shop. We are looking for partners.

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: Simulating a Dextrous Hand For Robotics With OpenUSD
description: Hands-on course on preparing robot USD assets for simulation, covering why physics tuning is foundational to robot learning, inspecting and configuring colliders and joints with OpenUSD/PhysX, and tuning control gains for stable, realistic motion. Participants work with a robot hand asset and apply best practices for simulation-ready authoring.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | AI for Spatial Storytelling: Going Beyond Generation with 3D World Models
description: Discover how 3D world models are poised to transform animation and visual effects by moving beyond visual generation toward systems that can understand, simulate, and reason about space and time. In this panel, Nikola Todorovic, Co-Founder of Wonder Dynamics, an Autodesk company, and Ben Mildenhall, Co-Founder of World Labs will explore why spatial intelligence and simulation matter for the next generation of creative tools. The conversation will connect long-term research ambitions with real-world production workflows. Presenters Ben Mildenhall World Labs Nikola Todorovic Autodesk

title: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage
description: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage is an interactive, generative art installation that re-imagines Los Angeles as a living, ever-evolving digital twin. Conceived for SIGGRAPH 2026, the project bridges LA’s rich cinematic legacy with the frontier of spatial computing. By seamlessly integrating Kinect motion tracking and AI-generated point clouds, it invites participants to co-author a collaborative digital monument. As users input their personal memories and desires of LA, individual landmarks dynamically merge, overlap, and fold into a singular, massive 3D cyber environment—blurring the boundaries between physical presence, collective memory, and cybernetic space. Credit： Visual & Interactive Director: Jimmz ZHANG, Digital FUN & TEA Community Technical Director: Ruokun CHEN, TEA Community & FLARE Lab Sound Artist: Louzhang, USC School of Cinematic Arts

title: Poster Sessions
description: New ideas start here. Join us to hear from authors as they discuss their work.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - LooseRoPE: Content-aware Attention Manipulation for Semantic Harmonization
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: LooseRoPE: Content-aware Attention Manipulation for Semantic Harmonization LooseRoPE enables prompt-free image editing by letting users crop and paste objects directly into new scenes. It improves diffusion-based harmonization by modulating RoPE with saliency, balancing identity preservation and contextual blending. This yields intuitive, spatially precise edits that maintain object appearance, pose, and placement without requiring text prompts or masks. Paper Presentation Presented: Thursday, July 23, 10:45 am-12:15 pm; Paper Session: Image Editing,; Room 408 B

title: Animation Theater 3: The Other Side of Quiet
description: Some stories don't announce themselves; they arrive in whispered confessions, long silences, and small acts of survival. This program gathers intimate works about the private weight of struggle and the unlikely ways people find their way through it.

title: Berthouzoz Women in Research Lunch
description: The Berthouzoz Women in Research Event is an annual networking event organized by WiGRAPH for researchers, faculty, and students. Floraine Berthouzoz started this event as an informal gathering. After her passing in 2015, Floraine's mentees and colleagues built upon her efforts to create an event that aims to broaden the network of women researchers and provide a friendly and personal environment where graduate students can interact with senior researchers. We will be hosting a panel of women in graphics who will share with us some of their research experiences. The event is open to all researchers, regardless of gender.

title: Toys Meet Tech: The Making of Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5 - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Chapters: West Coast Meetup
description: In conjunction with the Professional and Student Chapters Committee (PSCC), leaders from chapters on the West Coast of North America welcome you to join a social hour to connect with other students and professionals. This session will give information on chapters in the area, the activities they host, and ways to get involved. More information about the PSCC, chapters near you and how to get involved can be found at https://siggraph.org/chapters.

title: Fulbright Program International Exchange Opportunities
description: Are you interested in international exchange? Come be inspired and informed as a Fulbright alumnus describes his personal experience. Find out how you can develop a strong application for this prestigious academic and professional exchange program, now celebrating its 80th year. The Fulbright Program is the flagship international exchange program sponsored by the United States Government. In partnership with more than 160 countries worldwide, Fulbright offers an expansive suite of life-changing international academic and cultural exchange opportunities. Learn more at https://fulbrightprogram.org/apply/

title: Machine Learning, Neural Rendering, and the Future of Open Graphics
description: As AI and real time graphics continue to converge, the Khronos Group is helping build an open, cross platform foundation for scalable machine learning and neural graphics. Join us for an interactive discussion on insights from the Khronos AI Ecosystem Research Project, emerging industry trends, and the growing role of Vulkan and Vulkan ML in enabling portable AI acceleration. Explore use cases including neural shaders, on device inferencing, neural rendering, and efficient graphics pipelines. Bring your questions, share your perspective, and help shape the future of open AI for interactive 3D.

title: Neurodiversity in Computer Graphics, Games, and Interactive Media
description: Following community discussions at SIGGRAPH 2024 and 2025, this Birds of a Feather session invites educators, industry professionals, students, researchers, and advocates to discuss neurodiversity, accessibility, and inclusive practices across computer graphics, games, animation, and interactive media. Topics may include communication, collaboration, mentorship, workplace culture, learning environments, executive functioning, and sustainable support strategies. This session also provides an opportunity to discuss ongoing efforts toward building a Neurodiversity Affinity Group within the SIGGRAPH community and fostering year-round connection, advocacy, and visibility.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Practical Advances in AI-Accelerated Ray Tracing
description: Modern rendering is rapidly evolving through advances in GPU ray tracing and AI-assisted workflows. NVIDIA engineers will share recent OptiX and ray tracing developments shaping interactive and production renderers. Topics include AI-assisted denoising, DLSS integration, curve rendering, debugging and profiling, compile-time workflows, and support for larger, more dynamic, and more programmable rendering systems. The talk also examines evolving areas such as neural rendering, differentiable workflows, Gaussian splats, and memory-scalable scene management, with an emphasis on what is practical today and what is coming next. Presenters David Hart NVIDIA Tony Kanell NVIDIA

title: Virtual Theatre: Producing Live Theatre on Virtual Stages
description: In this panel, we explore the creation of live performance on virtual stages with participants in different physical locales. With a focus on production, we tap into the extensive experience of panelists working directly in this space and explore workflows, participant engagement, and the future of this kind of entertainment.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | A Small Team’s Journey to a VES Award-Winning Animated Film
description: Meet the young filmmakers behind Azimuth, a graduation short that went on to win the 24th Visual Effects Society Award for Outstanding Student Project, recognized for its artistry and innovation in animation. At a time when conversations around AI and humanity are everywhere, the film tells a deeply human story, asking: if technology could recreate someone you love, would it ever truly be enough? The young filmmakers will share how trust, problem-solving, and a shared love of storytelling helped them create something they never imagined possible together, and how they used Autodesk Maya and Arnold to layout, animate, and render the film under a tight timeline and with limited resources. Presenters Mathis Giraudeau Director ARTFX Damien Lagadeuc ARTFX

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - Abstraction in Style: Beyond Texture and Color
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: Abstraction in Style: Beyond Texture and Color Artistic styles often involve shape reinterpretation, not merely a change of surface appearance. Conventional style transfer methods miss this deeper abstraction behavior. We introduce Abstraction in Style (AiS), a two-stage generative framework that learns and transfers style at shape level, beyond texture and colors, enabling more expressive and controllable stylization. Paper Presentation Presented: Thursday, July 23, 9-10:30 am; Paper Session: NPR, Style & Abstraction; Room 408 B

title: Slang in Action: Modern Shading for Graphics, Compute, and AI
description: Join the Khronos Group, the Slang project team, and the developer community for an interactive look at the latest advances shaping Slang and modern programmable graphics pipelines. Explore recent project updates, emerging capabilities, real world workflows, and opportunities spanning graphics, compute, AI, and next generation rendering. Bring your questions, share your experiences, and engage directly with the developers and community helping shape what comes next for Slang.

title: Hands-on Course: Debugging USD composition
description: This hands-on course focuses on debugging USD composition in production. Participants gain practical experience authoring data across composition arcs and diagnosing issues in complex scenes. Building on USD in Production, the course emphasizes interactive use of modern debugging and visualization tools to understand how opinions, overrides, and data resolve.

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Blender, Oranges, and OVRTX (Not Your Typical Smoothie)
description: Blender is where many 3D ideas first take shape, and this course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp shows how to extend that familiar workspace with Omniverse libraries instead of moving the work somewhere else. Attendees will use AI agents to scaffold a Blender integration that connects ovrtx, ovphysx, and ovstage into a live creative workflow. The session walks through bringing GPU-accelerated path-traced rendering into Blender’s viewport.. Along the way, attendees will see how Blender’s scene graph can connect to Omniverse-powered rendering and simulation services. By the end, participants will have an RTX-rendered, physics-enabled scene running inside Blender, plus a clearer model for how composable Omniverse libraries can extend existing tools used by artists, technical directors, and simulation teams.

title: 4D Humans
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Art in the Time of A.I.
description: A discussion about ML/AI technology from an artist's perspective. Some of the issues that exist and where the new technology can help or hinder. Ultimately we will have to ask: what;s art, who;s an artist, and what's good/bad art? How does the use of ML/AI help or hinder the goal of making good art? Are we seeing an exciting new development in art or the downfall of creativity? Let's discuss: Not to convince others to think like we do but to learn from different points of view.

title: Cheap and Cheerful: Low-Fidelity Prototyping for Spatial Stories
description: This talk explores "cheap and cheerful" prototyping in immersive storytelling through the evolution of THE FOOTAGE, a VR narrative horror game. It traces how low-fidelity, no-code, and provisional methods surfaced crucial spatial insights, guided design decisions, enabled play-testing, and helped secure funding before full-scale production. Contributor Ethan Shaftel easyAction

title: Differentiable Geometry Processing & Flow Maps
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Film/VFX Production
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Generative AI Meets Computer Graphics
description: Generative AI is rapidly becoming a unifying interface for computer graphics, connecting large language models (LLMs), visual language models (VLMs), and video foundation models with geometry- and physics-aware representations. Led by experts from the University of Pennsylvania, MIT CSAIL, University of Utah, and UCLA, this workshop explores how modern generative models are reshaping the classical graphics pipeline, spanning 3D content creation, physics simulation, and animation. The program features a distinguished lineup of confirmed speakers from Stanford, the University of Chicago, Anuttacon, and Meshy AI, representing both academic pioneers and industry leaders. The primary objective is to address the fundamental challenges of deploying generative methods in real-world production, specifically focusing on the controllability and editability of generated content, physical plausibility and consistency over time, and practical integration into professional workflows for film, games, and CAD. Attendees can expect a highly interactive session structure that moves beyond theory into practical application. The workshop includes five 30-minute invited talks and two 15-minute lightning

title: Memory and Heritage
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Moving Mobile Graphics
description: A technical introduction to mobile graphics spanning the spectrum of hardware, software and developer experience, exploring the state of the art. We look at how neural graphics, GPU-driven rendering, global illumination and ray tracing techniques are being brought to mobile.

title: New Generations
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | GPU Rendering at Film Scale: Karma XPU in Production at Rodeo FX
description: Deploying GPU rendering across large-scale film pipelines requires balancing performance, scalability, and visual fidelity. In this session, SideFX and Rodeo FX will discuss Karma XPU’s capabilities in high-end production rendering. Mark Elendt will provide an overview of Karma XPU’s architecture and its integration of NVIDIA RTX technology, while Julien Héry and Scott Coates will examine practical applications from recent major productions. The session will cover GPU rendering at scale within an OpenUSD-based pipeline, from look development through final frame delivery. Presenters Mark Elendt SideFX Julien Héry Rodeo FX Scott Coates Rodeo FX

title: OpenXR: From XR Portability to Spatial Computing Platform
description: Join the Khronos Group, industry leaders, and the OpenXR community for an interactive session exploring how OpenXR is evolving beyond device interoperability to help power the next generation of intelligent, context aware spatial experiences. We will cover the Spatial Entity Framework, new spatial extensions, OpenXR 1.1, and the expanded Extension Matrix, now including middleware support visibility across Unity, Unreal, Godot, and more. Plus best practices for shipping across headsets, AR glasses, Android XR, mobile, and edge deployments. Bring your questions, share your perspective, and help shape what comes next for OpenXR.

title: Shading, Relighting and Volumes
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: The Universal Scribe: A Single Model for Every Writing System
description: Can one generative model synthesize images of text in every writing system, from Latin and Han to endangered minority scripts and ancient undeciphered writing? This informal meetup gathers people building generative models for text and document images: synthetic handwriting and calligraphy, font and glyph generation, training data for OCR and handwriting recognition, and digitizing the world's manuscripts. A central question: can one model generalize to scripts it never saw? Computer vision and generative AI researchers, type designers, document analysis and digital heritage folks, and underserved language communities are all welcome. Come argue, share data, and find collaborators.

title: Women at the Forefront: Discussing Storytelling in the Age of AI Production
description: Women at the Forefront is an interactive Birds of a Feather session built around open dialogue, networking, and shared exploration within the SIGGRAPH community. Through audience discussion, live Q&A, and candid industry conversation, attendees will engage directly with creative women shaping storytelling through AI, cloud production, and emerging digital workflows. Each panelist shares personal experiences, practical insight, and honest perspectives on navigating today’s rapidly evolving production landscape. Featuring Jieyi Lee, Jess Loren, Kim Landers, Liz Sloan Watts, and Jennette Vasiljevic, the session creates space for connection, collaboration, mentorship, and meaningful exchange across studios, brands, and the wider creative technology community.

title: World Wide Technology | Augmented, Not Automated
description: Generative AI does not require creative studios to throw away decades of tools, talent, and pipeline investment. The bigger opportunity is augmentation, not automation: keep the tools artists already know, keep the artists in creative control, and add agents as helpers inside the existing pipeline to take on the repetitive and technical work. This session shows how WWT, NVIDIA, and Cisco make that hybrid pipeline real and safe inside a real production stack, so AI accelerates the creative work without replacing the creator at the center of it. Presenters Kyle Lindsey World Wide Technology Rick Champagne NVIDIA

title: Building and Transitioning the Rainbow Affinity Group
description: The ACM SIGGRAPH Rainbow Affinity Group was formally established in June 2025 to support LGBTQ+ individuals in computer graphics and interactive techniques. Participants throughout the years of the Rainbow Meetup Birds of a Feather articulated strong interest in mentorship, networking, and inclusive programming. This session continues that discussion and provides a framework for action. This session presents the Rainbow Affinity Group as an active organizational design and transition effort. We reflect on early community input, examine the gap between ideation and execution, and introduce a structured model for forming a working committee to support future programming and leadership development. Through a facilitated discussion, attendees will be invited to help define priorities and identify pathways for participation. The session will initiate the formation of a new contributor cohort aligned with defined roles in programming, mentorship, communications, and industry engagement. This cohort will form the foundation for the group’s next phase and future leadership, in alignment with ACM SIGGRAPH governance practices.

title: Autodesk Vision Series | Animating a Multiverse of Horror for Marvel Zombies
description: Inside the making of Marvel Zombies, Stellar Creative Lab reveals the animation pipeline behind Marvel’s darkest animated universe yet. From eerie environments to stylized characters, discover the end-to-end production pipeline behind the #1 Disney+ series – spanning asset creation, layout, animation, lighting, lookdev, and final rendering for a fast-paced episodic workflow. Learn how the team used Maya for layout and animated performances, Arnold to achieve the show’s stylized horror look through toon shading, and Flow Production Tracking (formerly ShotGrid) to connect artists, assets, and production data across the studio. And discover how OpenUSD helped unify the pipeline, streamline collaboration, and support scalable production across the studio. Alongside exclusive production insights from Marvel Zombies, the team will discuss how Autodesk’s connected creative ecosystem enabled an artist-friendly workflow built for modern animated storytelling — and how emerging AI-powered workflows may help shape the future of animation production. Presenters Bert Van Brande Stellar Creative Lab Jordan Vanderzalm Stellar Creative Lab Nate Barnard Stellar Creative Lab

title: Sponsored Keynote by Tripo AI: Dr. Yanpei Cao, Chief Scientist, The Teapot Test: First It Tested Showing. Now It Tests Making.
description: The future of interactive media requires a fundamental shift: Moving from manually crafted environments to worlds generated on the fly by the imaginations of millions. Tripo’s core mission is to make this massive-scale, user-generated creation a reality. But to unlock this, we must rethink the entire generative pipeline, spanning from robust individual asset generation to the simulation of completely dynamic environments. In this deep-dive keynote, Tripo’s chief scientist and research scientists will share the unvarnished journey of building a generative ecosystem from the ground up. We will explore how Tripo scaled from preliminary fundamental research to a robust infrastructure currently serving millions of creators. The panel will first trace the rapid evolution of the generative landscape by dissecting Tripo’s full-stack approach to 3D generation. Through live demonstrations, the team will highlight key breakthroughs in geometry, topology, texturing, and rigging. These showcases will detail how we are bridging the gap between fundamental AI and graphics research and production-ready pipelines. However, generating individual assets is only half the equation. To unlock truly inte

title: Higgsfield AI | Kök Börü: An Artist-First Approach to AI in Animation
description: Kök Börü is a hybrid hand-drawn and AI-integrated animated short that tells a rare Central Asian story, the kind the traditional greenlight system tends to overlook. In this session, the team behind the film gives you the full picture in brief, covering why this story needed to exist, a condensed look at the hybrid pipeline that made it possible, and the real budget, team, and timeline numbers behind it. At its heart, though, this is just an honest conversation about AI and animation. We will look at where these tools genuinely help, where they fall short, and how they can open doors for underrepresented voices without pushing the artist out of the center of the work.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Real-Time Short Form Content: Behind The Scenes of the ILM Minis
description: In the fast paced world of short-form content, traditional rendering bottlenecks can stifle creativity, slow iteration, and limit use across media types. ILM’s Landis Fields and his team break down their real-time production pipeline that leverages GPU-accelerated workflows across each phase. Designed for creatives and technical directors alike, this session will walk through ILM’s process of creating stylized short form content. Everything from designing to executing a high-fidelity project entirely within a real-time ecosystem, drastically reducing render times, enabling live collaborative reviews, and empowering small teams to make creative decisions using a process that would otherwise be cost prohibitive.

title: REfact: Growing with Movement – A Hybrid Performance
description: REfract is a hybrid performance where a dancer and avatars coexist, evolve, and shift agency through motion capture and live rendering. In a 15-minute performance, set in a minimalist desert landscape with floating letters, the piece explores transformation, spatial perception, movement-driven storytelling, presence, autonomy, physical-digital interaction. Contributor Zelia ZZ Tan TechDanceLab

title: A New Media Techspressionist Architectures Experiment: An AR Mediated Speculative Expression in an Audio/Visual 21st century - LA World
description: As part of ACM SIGGRAPH DAC’s host city community outreach initiative, this project expands the New Media Architectures series through a collaborative cultural partnership within the City of Los Angeles and the Los Angeles Center for Digital Art (LACDA). Developed for SIGGRAPH LA 2026, the project positions Los Angeles itself as an experimental civic platform for artistic research, public engagement, and interdisciplinary collaboration across art, science, technology, and urban culture. This co-curated Augmented Reality experimental exhibition brings together architects, artists, composers, computer scientists, media artists, musicians, designers, and technologists to collaboratively develop responsive site-specific works. By engaging the city's cultural histories and communities, the project transforms LA into a living laboratory. The exhibition investigates how AI, autonomous systems, algorithmic governance, surveillance infrastructures, and machinic economies are reshaping contemporary urban life locally and globally. Through geospatial interventions and hybrid physical-digital experiences, the project proposes new curatorial and research models that rethink public and private s

title: From Cloud to Set: Discussing the Future of Cloud, Adaptive, and AI-Native Production
description: Cloud to Set: The Future of Adaptive and AI-Native Production is an exciting BOF session at SIGGRAPH featuring an award-winning panel of filmmakers, technologists, and creative innovators shaping the future of production. Panelists Erik Weaver (USC ETC), Erick Geisler (Global Objects, G2 Immersive Studios), and Christopher Nichols (Chaos Innovation Lab, Monstrous Moonshine) bring decades of combined experience across Emmy-winning work, groundbreaking VFX, and pioneering AI-native and cloud-based production pipelines. Designed as a BOF, the session emphasizes open dialogue, networking, and shared exploration, with dedicated Q&A and industry insights exchanged between panelists and attendees through discussion.

title: Hands-on Course: Sponsored by Autodesk: From Footage to Previs: AI Mocap Workflows with Autodesk Flow Studio and Maya
description: This hands-on course is designed for professional animation and VFX artists with experience using Autodesk Maya who want to explore new approaches to previsualization and early production. Using Autodesk Flow Studio, participants will work with live-action footage to generate editable exports such as AI mocap, camera tracking, character passes, clean plates, full 3D scenes, and more. These elements will be used as a foundation for exploring performance, camera movement, and spatial storytelling before heavy asset development begins. The workshop focuses on how early creative exploration can inform layout, shot design, and production planning. Through practical examples and guided walkthroughs, participants will understand how Flow Studio can support early iteration and integrate with Maya to establish creative intent and a production-ready foundation. Learning Objectives By the end of the hands-on course, participants will be able to: · Use Flow Studio to generate editable mocap data, camera tracking, character passes, clean plates and full 3D scenes from live-action footage · Apply Flow Studio outputs to previsualization and layout workflows in Maya · Explore performance, camera m

title: NVIDIA Hands-On Lab: Isaac Sim and the Fine Art of Making Your Robot Pick Things Up
description: This hands-on course in the NVIDIA Physical AI Agent Bootcamp turns an empty USD stage into a working Isaac Sim robot scene, with AI agents acting as simulation setup partners. Attendees will import a robot manipulator from URDF, compose the scene using prior lab assets or user-selected environments, and bring SimReady content into Isaac for testing. The session focuses on the practical path from “nothing is in the scene” to “the robot picked up the cube.” Participants will configure the robot, prepare the object, validate physics behavior, tune the scene, and execute a basic pick-and-place task. Agent skills handle repeatable setup steps so attendees can focus on scene intent, simulation checks, and task behavior. By the end, attendees will have a composed Isaac Sim scene with a physics-validated robot, interactive assets, and a successful pick-and-place task that connects OpenUSD scene building to robotics simulation.

title: The State of Vulkan: Updates, Tools, and Developer Resources
description: Join the Vulkan Working Group for an update on the latest developments across the Vulkan ecosystem. Vulkan Working Group Chair Ralph Potter will discuss the group's direction, recent accomplishments, and upcoming work. Additional presentations will cover descriptor heaps, Vulkan debugging and developer tools, and an overview of available Vulkan tutorials and learning resources. Whether you are new to Vulkan or an experienced developer, this BOF is an opportunity to learn about current best practices, new capabilities, and the future of the API directly from the experts helping shape it.

title: World Models for Robotics: Bridging Graphics, Simulation, and Physical Intelligence
description: World models are rapidly emerging as a unifying paradigm across graphics, simulation, spatial intelligence, embodied AI, and robotics. This Birds of a Feather brings together researchers, engineers, founders, and students to discuss the latest advances in generative world models, digital twins, neural simulation, 3D foundation models, active perception, and robot learning. Through short introductions and open discussion, participants will explore how graphics and simulation communities can help accelerate the development of scalable physical AI systems. All perspectives and backgrounds are welcome. Organized by Saturday Robotics, an open forum connecting researchers and practitioners across embodied AI, world models, and physical intelligence.

title: Emerging Technologies Demo - A Generalizable Light Transport 3D Embedding for Global Illumination
description: This Emerging Technologies demo is a partner presentation to the Technical Paper: A Generalizable Light Transport 3D Embedding for Global Illumination We introduce a scalable 3D embedding that learns global illumination directly from 3D scene configurations. This design scales to environments with millions of triangles, enabling the first generalizable GI learning on complex, high-fidelity indoor scenes, far beyond prior limits. It bypasses per-scene retraining and screen-space artifacts to deliver realistic rendering. Paper Presentation Presented: Tuesday, July 21, 2-3:30 pm; Paper Session: Light Transport; Room 408 A

title: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage
description: LA Folded: A Cybernetic Mirage is an interactive, generative art installation that re-imagines Los Angeles as a living, ever-evolving digital twin. Conceived for SIGGRAPH 2026, the project bridges LA’s rich cinematic legacy with the frontier of spatial computing. By seamlessly integrating Kinect motion tracking and AI-generated point clouds, it invites participants to co-author a collaborative digital monument. As users input their personal memories and desires of LA, individual landmarks dynamically merge, overlap, and fold into a singular, massive 3D cyber environment—blurring the boundaries between physical presence, collective memory, and cybernetic space. Credit： Visual & Interactive Director: Jimmz ZHANG, Digital FUN & TEA Community Technical Director: Ruokun CHEN, TEA Community & FLARE Lab Sound Artist: Louzhang, USC School of Cinematic Arts

title: MiniMax | New Frontier in Multimodality
description: Driven by the mission to co-create intelligence with everyone, MiniMax is dedicated to accelerating the path to AGI. Through independently developed foundation models, MiniMax enables the seamless integration of text, audio, image, video, and music. This session will unveil a new frontier in multimodality, exploring how these cross-modal capabilities redefine the future of next-generation creative workflows and intelligent experiences.

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Building a GPU Production Renderer with OptiX at Netflix Animation Studios
description: Maintaining real-time interactivity while navigating complex, large-scale animation environments is a major challenge for production pipelines. In this session, Netflix Animation Studios will share its journey building Glimpse, a custom, fully OpenUSD-based ray tracing viewport using NVIDIA OptiX, designed to replace standard viewports such as Maya Viewport 2.0. The presentation will cover how the team addresses large-scale scene complexity, optimizes USD memory sharing, and supports native instancing for large production scenes. Attendees will learn how this high-performance, GPU-accelerated viewport was designed for fast adoption in production workflows.

title: Bringing The Mandalorian and Grogu to Life: Virtual Production, Creatures, and VFX Innovations
description: Industrial Light & Magic invites you behind the scenes of The Mandalorian and Grogu for an in-depth exploration of the visual effects that brought the latest Star Wars feature to life. In this panel, the filmmakers and artists will unpack how groundbreaking real-time technology, virtual production workflows, and time-tested VFX techniques came together to create immersive worlds, unforgettable characters, and epic action on the big screen. From on-set innovation to final pixels, join the ILM team as they share creative insights, technical breakthroughs, and lessons learned while pushing the boundaries of modern visual storytelling. Presenters John Knoll Executive Creative Director/Sr. VFX Supervisor Industrial Light & Magic Hal Hickel Animation Supervisor Industrial Light & Magic Rebecca Forth Senior Look Development Lighting Technical Director Industrial Light & Magic

title: NVIDIA RTX Rendering Day | Advancing LED Virtual Production with Neural Rendering
description: Achieving high frame rates and visual fidelity on LED stages requires continuous pipeline innovation. This session explores how NVIDIA DLSS technologies, including Frame Generation, Super Resolution, and Ray Reconstruction, can deliver performance and quality gains for Unreal Engine-based virtual production. Markus Hoell will discuss how real-time AI inference can offload heavy rendering workloads, along with real-world developer stories covering engineering challenges, pipeline hurdles, future DLSS architecture considerations, and lessons learned from adapting neural rendering technologies for LED environments.

title: AI-Powered Standards: How Khronos Is Building the Future of Graphics
description: From specification authorship to community engagement, AI is reshaping how standards bodies like Khronos operate. This panel brings together Khronos Working Group leaders and industry contributors to explore how AI tools are accelerating standards development, improving documentation quality, and lowering barriers for community participation. Panelists will discuss real-world applications — from AI-assisted spec review and conformance testing to smarter developer outreach and feedback analysis. Attendees are invited to join the conversation and share how AI is influencing their own standards workflows. Come ready to ask questions and help shape what's next.

title: Appy Hour
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: Everyone Is an Obscure Artist: Living Lineages in Computational Creativity
description: Fame is an edge case. Obscurity is the normal condition of creative work. Important creative technology often appears first as demos, tools, performances, installations, prototypes, virtual worlds, and interactive systems — not papers. Even when formally presented or archived, these works can disappear from the field’s working memory because research culture transmits papers better than living artifacts. This BOF invites artists, researchers, tool-builders, educators, and independent creators to discuss how experimental work becomes legible, transmissible, and buildable across computer graphics, AI, XR, animation, games, and computational performance. The goal is continuity: a living support network for work carried forward.

title: Bringing The Mandalorian and Grogu to Life: Virtual Production, Creatures, and VFX Innovations - Production Session Meet & Greet
description: Join us for a meet and greet with our speakers right after the session ends. This is a fantastic chance to continue the conversation, network with fellow attendees, and ask any questions you may have. You won't want to miss it!

title: Khronos Group Networking Reception
description: Cap off a full day of Khronos Working Group updates and technical deep-dives with the annual Khronos Networking Reception! Join fellow developers, artists, and industry leaders for an evening of great food, drinks, and live sponsor demos. Whether you want to connect with the people behind the standards, explore the latest from our sponsors, or simply recharge with the community after a packed day of BOFs, this is the place to be. All are welcome!

title: Electronic Theater (separate ticket required)
description: Every year the Electronic Theater highlights a selection of the very best in computer animation and this year's edition continues in this tradition. The prestigious, Oscar® qualifying festival is your ticket to this best, and soon to be buzziest, short film projects. Opening night also includes the presentation of the festival awards!

title: Frontier Talk: UNIX V4 and the Utah Teapot
description: We recently recovered UNIX V4 from a 1974 magnetic tape at the University of Utah. This version of the UNIX operating system, thought to have been lost, was the 19th copy distributed to the public, just months after the first public announcement. It was originally acquired by Martin Newell while managing the computer graphics laboratory, and it was likely connected to his foundational research in procedural modeling and the famous Utah teapot. This talk shows the unique insight this artifact provides into the dawn of computer graphics and the history of UNIX.

title: Feathers, Scales and Curls
agenda: See the official SIGGRAPH program page for the full abstract, agenda, and assets.

title: From Human Vision to Machine Vision
description: Visual computing encompasses the full range of technologies and methodologies involved in rendering, displaying, reconstructing, modeling, and recording visual data. Among the many subfields within visual computing, there has been a growing interest in two subfields in particular: human vision and machine vision. Human vision research focuses on understanding how human vision and cognition functions and using that knowledge to improve the user experience through realism, immersion, and responsiveness. In contrast, machine vision research focuses on building computational systems that complete a visual perception task, and may aim to mimic the structure or functionality of biological vision systems. This workshop aims to facilitate dialogue and collaboration between researchers and practitioners in machine and human vision. By exploring common challenges, methodologies, and philosophical approaches, ranging from perceptually grounded models to data-driven deep learning frameworks, we hope to spark interdisciplinary discussion that drives innovation across the broader field of visual computing.

title: Frontiers Workshop: Dronevision, Holodecks, and Spatial Computing Using Swarms of Flying Light Specks
description: This timely SIGGRAPH 2026 Frontiers workshop explores Flying Light Specks (FLSs), including their applications, current capabilities, and future research directions. An FLS is a miniature drone equipped with a lighting apparatus that serves as a building block for next-generation display systems. These systems range from desktop Dronevision platforms to room-scale holodecks and outdoor spatial-computing environments. Swarms of FLSs can illuminate graphics and animations visible to the naked eye, enabling users to interact with them using their bare hands. Topics include the classification of FLS devices with LightBender as a representative example, end-to-end architectures spanning content generation, illumination, and bare-hand interaction. For more information visit: https://www.flsworkshop.com

title: Geospatial and 3D Tiles
description: In this session we'll cover how glTF is being used for runtime-efficient streaming of 3D geospatial data and digital twins. This includes a wide range of content types like Gaussian splats, point clouds, vector data, volumetric data, and AEC/CAD/BIM. We'll also look at glTF 2.1's new complex scenes capabilities and how this scales to massive datasets.

title: How to Get Your Resume & Reel Noticed
description: The ACM SIGGRAPH Early Career Development Committee’s “Resume and Reel Review” program has long offered students and early career professionals the chance to have their work reviewed by industry experts at SIGGRAPH conferences. This session will feature a panel of industry professionals who will discuss and review a selection from this year’s program live. This unique opportunity allows attendees to see real-time reviews, learn what makes a great resume and reel, and ask questions directly to experts. Register for a one-on-one session at https://ecdc.siggraph.org.

title: NVIDIA | The Making of The Great Galactic War: Directing Generative Video in 3D
description: The Great Galactic War, a short-form streaming series from Nick Montori, Co-Founder and Creative Director of AI production house Anima Studios, has surpassed 120 million views. This session shows how a small team created a consistent, cinematic world at streaming scale, and the virtual cinematography tool that made it possible. Like many teams working with generative video, Anima faced a core challenge: control and consistency. Foundation models can produce striking frames, but camera movement, composition, lighting, and scene continuity can shift unpredictably between renders, making it difficult to direct a coherent series. Using real production footage, Pinar Seyhan Demirdağ and Dade Orgeron will show how the team used EVRN, a 3D directing layer for generative video, to solve that problem. They will demonstrate how a flat image becomes an editable 3D scene where characters, props, and environments become independent objects with accurate spatial relationships. The team could then place cameras, light scenes, compose shots, and lock layouts before sending them to the generative model, replacing prompt-driven guesswork with deterministic references for repeatable renders. Attendee

title: NVIDIA: Advanced Real-Time Volume Graphics
description: This course teaches attendees how to write sci-vis volume renderers on modern architectures. Since the last course at Eurographics 2006 the hardware and algorithmic landscape have changed tremendously. We show how this has influenced the state-of-the-art in real-time volume graphics in the last years.

description: Come meet the WOSC team and share your thoughts on what we should focus on in the coming year! We want to hear from you on the types of events, topics, speakers, and types of community engagement we can help build within the SIGGRAPH community.

title: Trial by Fire: The VFX of The Lost Bus
description: In this Production Session, beloFX takes audiences inside the making of the Oscar-nominated visual effects of The Lost Bus, where real-time workflows, large-scale simulation, and cinematic storytelling came together. Built around director Paul Greengrass’ visceral handheld style, the film demanded an immersive and grounded approach to recreating California’s devastating 2018 Camp Fire. Using Unreal Engine, the team developed a large-scale “digital twin” of Northern California for previs, virtual scouting, and production planning, while game-inspired workflows powered everything from dense traffic simulations to reactive handheld camera movement. The session will explore how these real-time systems helped the production rapidly adapt when several sequences unexpectedly shifted into full CG midway through filming due to real world wildfires. Attendees will gain insight into scalable previs, resilient pipelines, and the evolving role of game-engine technology in modern VFX filmmaking. Presenters Zoe Cranley Head of CG beloFX Phil Bonner Asset Supervisor beloFX

title: Art Gallery
description: The SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery explores computer graphics and interactive techniques as languages and materials to express artistic visions and experiences. The theme “In-Betweens” highlights the interactions that the twelve installations establish with space, time, and generations. Across media and approaches, including screens, projections, installations, sculptures, retro devices, wearables, bio art, data visualization art, robotics, and space art, the Art Gallery brings together creators whose works address large-scale questions and humanitarian concerns; investigate foundational components such as algorithmic methods and computing hardware; expand how we perceive an increasingly interconnected world; and re-examine and speculate about our place within the layered infrastructures of contemporary technology.

title: No-Code Virtual Reality & Immersive Learning
description: How are educators creating immersive learning experiences without coding? This Birds of a Feather session brings together educators, researchers, learning designers, and XR practitioners to share ideas, tools, and workflows for building virtual reality experiences, immersive 360° tours, and digital twins. Participants will discuss successful projects, challenges, and emerging opportunities while exploring platforms that make immersive content creation more accessible than ever. Featuring examples from RMIT University, Australia, this interactive meetup encourages knowledge sharing, networking, and collaboration. Join the conversation, showcase your work, discover new approaches, and help shape the future of immersive education.

title: NVIDIA | The Making of Kök Börü: Where Untold Stories Find New Pipelines
description: Kök Börü is a hybrid hand-drawn and AI-integrated animated short that brings a rare Central Asian story to life, one that traditional studio greenlight processes often overlook. In this panel, the creative team pulls back the curtain on their artist-first pipeline, commenting on how to meaningfully integrate AI tools to augment rather than replace traditional craftsmanship. They will share unfiltered budget, team, and timeline metrics, offering a transparent, honest account of what worked, what broke, and how they built an unconventional production model from the ground up.

title: Remote Production in 2026: What actually works?
description: Remote production is no longer experimental. Across animation, VFX and real-time studios, distributed workflows are now central to production operations. Yet key questions remain: which remote and hybrid models are sustainable, and how can studios balance security, infrastructure costs, operational complexity and access to global talent? This Birds of a Feather session brings together technology leaders from animation studios and the production technology ecosystem to share lessons learned from large-scale deployments. Topics include workstation access, multi-site production, cloud and hybrid infrastructure, pipeline operations, security and evolving remote technologies. Attendees will compare experiences and identify effective industry practices.

title: Dog Walk: Narrating Human-AI Alignment through Companion Robots
description: BFF: Dog Walk explores human-AI intimacy through co-parenting two robot dogs. With video and live performance, the artist-researchers challenge the mind-body divide, documenting the evolution of their dogs' LLMs and their life in the intimate everyday. Blending human and machine perspectives, this project seeks a hybrid language exploring the boundaries of authenticity, embodiment, and synthetic companionship.

title: Hands-on Course: NVIDIA: From 3D Captures to Simulated Digital Environments
description: We introduce a novel capture-to-simulation pipeline for in-the-wild 3D Gaussian Splat scenes, showing our research-based solutions for segmenting objects, predicting their volumetric mechanical properties using a recent learning-based method, and mixed splat-mesh physics simulations, all supported by recent features in the Kaolin Library.

title: ACM SIGGRAPH Digital Arts Community Roundtables
description: Art Community Roundtables for Art Papers, Art Gallery, and Digital Arts Community members. Community members will be invited to join conversations related to the Art Gallery, Art Papers, and Digital Arts Community activities, as well as other themes related to the arts at SIGGRAPH.

title: Beyond the Score: Interpreting Graphics Benchmark Results for Better System Decisions
description: Graphics benchmark scores are often used to compare systems, evaluate hardware, and guide purchasing decisions, but results are easier to quote than to interpret. Their meaning depends on the workload, system configuration, and bottlenecks behind the measurement. This talk explores how workload design, display resolution, system balance, driver behavior, and software optimization shape measured graphics performance, and how average scores can obscure behavior that affects real-world user experience. Attendees will learn how to ask better questions of benchmark data, recognize what results do and do not represent, and apply them more effectively when comparing systems and selecting the right configuration.

title: Access:VFX Networking + Recruiter AMA
description: ACCESS:VFX is a global non-profit of over 70 leading Animation and VFX studios. We focus on actively pursuing and encouraging inclusion, diversity, awareness and opportunity. Step away from your screens and join Access:VFX IRL! While our monthly virtual networking sessions are great, nothing compares to connecting and engaging face-to-face with our amazing community. This inclusive space is your opportunity to meet-up, share experiences, and build your network. Plus, we’re hosting an exclusive "Ask VFX Recruiters Anything" session. Bring your questions and get honest, real-world advice on how to stand out and navigate the complexities and challenges of the industry today.

title: AI in Education: Continuing Conversations on Disruptive Trends
description: This BOF session builds on discussions from the 2025 Educators’ Forum panel, which examined the challenges educators face in addressing key industry trends. The 2026 session will feature a facilitated discussion among organizers and attendees, revisiting major disruptors and trends in: (1) AI in education, (2) industry readiness and partnerships, and (3) evolving learning modalities.

title: NVIDIA | Making the Old Stories New: Innovative Dreams and Moses
description: The Old Stories: Moses revisits one of the most enduring stories ever told. In this talk, Innovative Dreams shares how its artists and filmmakers approached the making of Moses, using real-time hybrid filmmaking to move from imagination to image with greater speed and flexibility. The team connects performance, virtual environments, and real-time creative iteration to shape atmosphere, scale, and emotional clarity. The session is less about technology as spectacle, and more about the craft of making an ancient story feel immediate, intimate, and alive. Presenters Jon Erwin WONDER PROJECT; INNOVATIVE DREAMS Tim Moore Vū Technologies

title: "Dear Upstairs Neighbors": Artist-Centered, AI-Assisted Expressionistic Animation
description: A young woman named Ada, desperate for a good night’s sleep, is kept awake by her exceedingly noisy neighbors. As she struggles to imagine what could be causing the cacophony upstairs, reality drifts into fantasy, and an epic battle for peace and sanity ensues. That’s the story of “Dear Upstairs Neighbors”, an animated short made by a hybrid team of animation veterans and AI researchers. The artistic core of the film — storyboards, concept art, character animation — was lovingly hand-crafted by artists using traditional 2D and 3D techniques. Our technical artists then created custom generative image and video models, which they fine-tuned on our own concept art and used, much like a renderer, to unite these hand-crafted components into polished shots that respect all of the artistic choices made along the way. The film’s expressionistic painterly style evolves, shot by shot, to reflect Ada’s intense and changing emotions. To achieve this evolving look by hand would have meant painting over 7,000 frames, with artists spending so much effort on execution that they would have no time to iterate beyond a first pass. To do it using computer graphics would have required developing dozens

title: The Instrument Is the Story: Ten Years of Spatial Music-Making with Paradiddle
description: In this demo, we trace the ten-year development of Paradiddle, a VR drumming application, showing how changes in the XR landscape shaped its evolution. We explore how our studio's guiding design questions adapted to a rapidly changing medium and the users that changed with it. Contributor Emre Tanirgan Paradiddle XR

title: Hands-on Course: Advance World Simulation With 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Environment Reconstruction
description: Learn how to reconstruct a large scene for robotics testing using NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec Gaussian-based reconstruction technologies to perform multi-GPU training, object segmentation, and extraction. This lab will walk through core concepts with a step-by-step workflow for data capture, reconstruction, and object level-integration for robotics simulation in NVIDIA Isaac Lab.

title: Techspressionism: An Expressive Approach to Digital Art
description: Techspressionism: what is it? An artistic approach in which technology is utilized as a means to express emotional experience. Its practitioners form a global network of artists spanning over 45 countries. This presentation highlights current activities, including exhibitions at the LA Center for Digital Art (LACDA) and the Arda Khiva Museum in Uzbekistan, as well as past exhibitions and initiatives. We also discuss our monthly virtual salons, artist interviews, roundtables, and weekly coworking sessions. Additionally, we will share details about an exciting new collaboration between Techspressionism and SIGGRAPH DAC that is on display during SIGGRAPH2006.

description: After four days of education-focused presentations, attendees are invited to an invigorating, open discussion on current topics in computer graphics education and interactive techniques. Come to the town hall session to ask questions, share perspectives, and have a good time with the SIGGRAPH education community!

title: SIGGRAPH 2026 Technical Papers Town Hall
description: Town Hall discussion of the SIGGRAPH Technical Papers Program.

title: History: Fueling the Future by Preserving the Past - The ACM SIGGRAPH History Archive
description: If you are interested in the history of computer graphics, this session is for you. The ACM SIGGRAPH History Archive is both a physical collection and an online archive of SIGGRAPH organization leadership and events, conference presentations, demonstrations, animation screenings, art exhibitions, and more. The archive is a volunteer effort involving pioneers, professionals, students, and community members. This session will showcase the new additions to the archive and include short talks by student interns and volunteers. We will also focus on how this incredibly information-rich archive has fueled the future of computer graphics research and creative endeavors. The archive has grown tremendously and now includes more than 75,000 pages of information. One of the most significant contributions to the community is opening up the archive so the AI bots could learn from 52 years worth of conference presentations, demonstrations, animation screening, etc. Today, Gemini, ChatGPT and other AI assistants often cite the ACM SIGGRAPH History Archive and the information related to the history of computer graphics is much more accurate. Another significant addition was the programming of an o

title: NVIDIA | Making ChikaBOOM!: AI, Animation, and the Artist-Centered Production Pipeline
description: ChikaBOOM! offers a timely case study in how generative AI is being used inside a real animation production pipeline. Written and directed by c. Craig Patterson, the short follows aspiring young magician Chika Batstone as she accidentally unleashes a chaotic magical creature into New York City. In this session, Patterson will unpack how AI supported the making of the film, from creative development and visual exploration through production iteration and final frames. The session will focus on the practical realities of AI-assisted production: what worked, how teams maintained creative control, and what lessons apply to studios, artists, technologists, and production leaders building AI into animation workflows. Presenters Benjamin Michel Asteria Tian Pei LTX c. Craig Patterson Filmmaker

title: Smells Like Memory: Reconstructing Virtual Autobiographical Spaces Through Scent in VR
description: An immersive VR experience investigates spatial storytelling through the integration of olfactory memory and photographic interaction. By framing photography as a tangible interface for memory recall, the work examines how scent-driven, embodied interaction enables non-linear autobiographical reconstruction and contributes to research on multi-sensory narrative design in virtual environments. Contributor Jiaxuan Yin Independent

title: The Urgency of Now: Digital Art For Democracy!
description: Even if you've never made protest art before: now is the time to step up. We'll discuss the recent surge of creative resistance in the face of tyranny, with reviews of both recent and upcoming pro-democracy art shows and emphasis on how we can all get more involved. We'll encourage SIGGRAPH artists to stand up and refuse to remain silent, to use creative practice as vital weapon of resistance and tool for civic awakening: celebrating the power of art to unite, heal, and defend our shared freedoms using bold visual dissent against authoritarianism. Contact: art4democracy@isagordon.net for more info.

title: Frontiers Workshop: Form and Function: A Hands-On Workshop on Computationally Designing and Fabricating Barrier-Grid Animations
description: This frontiers workshop equips participants with hands-on experience using a modern computational design workflow for creating and fabricating barrier-grid animations. Participants will author animations whose occlusion patterns are shaped by mathematical functions, preview them under multiple interaction modes, and fabricate take-home artifacts. By participating in this workshop, attendees will understand how to design expressive barrier-grid animations that balance form and function.

title: NVIDIA | The Making of Sentinel: How AI Infrastructure Tools are Reshaping Film Production
description: Sentinel, an upcoming stop-motion feature film by Academy Award-winning visual effects and animation pioneer Phil Tippett, will serve as a lens for exploring a surreal alternate universe within a soldier's mind during World War I. Drawing on Tippett's legendary visual storytelling virtuosity, the project reflects an agentic AI-first production workflow and creative process where ideas evolve through exploration and experimentation. In this talk, the artists behind Sentinel discuss how story, intuition, and collaboration guide the work, and how their partnership with LOFTAPPS' LOFTai AI infrastructure platform helps manage assets, provenance and resources while preserving creative intent as the film takes shape. Presenters Phil Tippet Tippett Studio Remko Noteboom LOFTAPPS Gary Mundell LOFTAPPS

title: NVIDIA: A Step-by-Step Introduction to the ANARI Rendering API
description: This course introduces the Khronos ANARI rendering API using a highly practical, step-by-step approach in which attendees learn to write increasingly capable ANARI based applications. At the end of this course, users should be easily able to realize their own 3D applications using ANARI for both interactive and offline rendering use cases.

title: Performance, Rendering, and Interaction for Competitive Esports (PRICE)
description: The Performance, Rendering, and Interaction for Competitive Esports (PRICE) workshop brings together cutting-edge research on topics essential to esports, including graphics and rendering, systems and performance, human factors and HCI, computer supported cooperative work (CSCW), data, and artificial intelligence. Esports is no longer a niche hobby; it is a major cultural and economic force, with a global audience in the hundreds of millions and a market size exceeding $1 billion. The performance and experience of both players and spectators are critically dependent on the underlying technology. Success in competitive games hinges on a complex interplay between hardware capabilities, software optimizations, and the limits of human perception, performance, and experience. The program includes a keynote, sketch presentations, and interactive panel discussions among sketch authors where the audience will be able to participate in the discussion. Additionally, a few short breaks in the agenda will allow for small group and one-on-one interactions. Technical Workshop Sketches: AI Level of Detail: Distance-Aware ML Model Precision Selection for Real-Time Human Motion Prediction in Games

title: Physics Is Easier Than You Think: From Classical to Neural Elastic Simulation
description: Through theory and reference code, attendees will learn the simple foundations of elastic simulation. Once the basics are established, the course further explores through the current research landscape of neural physics simulation, showing how newcomers can integrate modern physics simulation into their projects.

title: Remesher: Automate Character Pipelines with ComfyUI's API, Docker, and Agents
description: Learn to build automated character generation pipelines using ComfyUI's REST/WebSocket APIs. Deploy state-of-the-art open source image generation and 3d generation models (Qwen, Hunyuan3D) via Docker and cloud GPU providers, then build Typer CLI tools that transform text prompts into rigged 3D characters through programmatic workflow execution: generate→turnaround→mesh→rig.

title: SYNAPTICON
description: SYNAPTICON is an artistic research artifact at the intersection of neuro-hacking, brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), and foundation models, probing new territories of human expression, aesthetics, and AI alignment. Contributor Albert Barque-Duran IASLab (Interactive Arts & Science Lab) - La Salle Barcelona (Universitat Ramon Llull)

title: The Pipeline Conference - Organizer Meetup
description: The Pipeline Conference will be in-person in September in Vancouver, Canada. We will discuss future pipeline focused events and the organization as a whole. Come meet The Pipeline Conference organizers. Get involved. Share your dream pipeline ideas for upcoming events. Join us and participate in the conversation.

title: World Models: When Digital Reality Thinks, Adapts and Responds
description: World models could redefine how games, animation, film and television are imagined, explored and created. Emerging across creative media, simulation and robotics, world models transcend fixed environments and programmed rules by learning how reality behaves, maintaining context, predicting outcomes and responding to creative or user input. This facilitated discussion with developers, filmmakers, artists, researchers and production technologists examines the opportunities, limitations and practical requirements of this emerging technology. We will explore persistence, memory, causality, directability, consistency and control, asking what skills are needed and what new forms of creation become possible when digital reality can think, adapt and respond.

title: Archiving Media Art and Preserving Obsolete Technologies
description: How do museums, archives, and libraries steward media artworks built on obsolete or rapidly aging technologies? From CD-ROMs and Flash to early VR headsets, custom hardware, and platform-dependent installations, much of recent media art history runs on systems no longer manufactured or supported. This Birds of a Feather, convened with the LA Media Art Archives Network, brings together archivists, conservators,curators, artists, and technologists to share institutional approaches. Topics may include emulation, migration, documentation, artist interviews, exhibition and archival standards, and the realities of long-term stewardship of media art. Come share challenges and solutions from your own practice. All levels welcome.

title: An Introduction to the Fourier Transform
description: The Fourier Transform is fundamental to computer graphics, explaining topics from aliasing and sampling to image compression and filtering. This friendly course explains the principles in words, pictures, and animation, rather than math. The concepts are the important thing. We show that they are comprehensible, useful, and beautiful.
